| 19  | `/next-task` skill (queue-mode loop)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Fixed-priority preemptive scheduling                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Liu & Layland, "Scheduling Algorithms for Multiprogramming in a Hard Real-Time Environment", _JACM_ 1973; Liu, _Real-Time Systems_, 2000                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Discrete priority levels (P0–P3) instead of numerical; preemption is at task boundaries (cooperative), not interrupt-level. **Why acceptable:** task durations are minutes-to-hours, so cooperative scheduling has no observable cost; discrete levels match human triage.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 20  | TypeScript strict++ + Biome + lefthook                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Defensive programming + early-failure / fail-fast                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Hunt & Thomas, _The Pragmatic Programmer_, 1999 (Tip 32 "crash early"); Lampson 1983 (above) hint "use exceptions only for exceptional conditions"; Beck, _Extreme Programming Explained_, 1999 (continuous integration)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Strict types catch defects at compile; Biome catches style/complexity at lint; lefthook fails fast on commit. The constraint (rule #6) is moved to the cheapest possible point. | 21  | The `(@agent-id)` claim convention in TASKS.md                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Lease                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Gray & Cheriton, "Leases: An Efficient Fault-Tolerant Mechanism for Distributed File Cache Consistency", _SOSP_ 1989                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Time-bounded exclusive access. **Deviation:** no explicit timeout; staleness is detected by the picker rule "Found + stale (no related code) → unclaim". **Why acceptable:** humans + git history are the timeout signal. | 22  | This index                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Pattern language / pattern catalogue                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Alexander et al., _A Pattern Language_, 1977; Gabriel, _Patterns of Software_, 1996                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | A living catalogue indexed by artifact, anchored in source, with explicit conformance level — the form pattern languages take in software architecture per Gabriel.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| 23  | Constitutional rule #9 + the per-task measurement contract                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Goal-Question-Metric + pre-registration                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Basili, Caldiera, Rombach, "The Goal-Question-Metric Approach", _Encyclopedia of Software Engineering_ 1994; Munafò et al., "A Manifesto for Reproducible Science", _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (pre-registration); Fagerholm et al., "Building Blocks for Continuous Experimentation", _RCoSE_ 2014                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Goal = success criterion. Question = "what would tell us the goal is being met?". Metric = the OTEL query / shell command in the measurement-method cell. Pre-registration (committing the prediction before observing) is what makes the experiment falsifiable; pivot threshold operationalises Ries 2011's pivot-or-persevere. Iron rule: no exemption for bugfixes — preparation PRs land the metric source first when missing.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 24  | `@minsky/observability` (`Observability` interface + `OtelObservability` strategy)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Adapter (structural) + Strategy (behavioral); three-signal observability (traces / metrics / logs)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Gamma et al., _Design Patterns_, 1994; OpenTelemetry specification (CNCF 2020+)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | OTEL implementation deviates from the standard convention by _not_ registering its providers globally (`trace.setGlobalTracerProvider`, etc.) — see the package README's "v0 deviation declared" section. Restoring full conformance: a single explicit `setup.sh`-driven global registration at process start, tracked as future task `register-otel-globals-at-bootstrap`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 25  | `@minsky/token-monitor` (`TokenMonitor` interface + `StubTokenMonitor`)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Adapter + Strategy; test double / fake                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Gamma et al. 1994; Meszaros, _xUnit Test Patterns_, 2007 (test double / fake)                                                                                                                                                      | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Interface + in-memory stub for tests. Real Strategy against Maciek's `claude-monitor` Python tool ships in `budget-guard-maciek-impl` follow-up. Diff-based — existing catch chains are grandfathered until the file is modified again (rule-1 / rule-3 precedent). Pure function (`checkOtelCoverage`) over `{ files: { path, source }[] }` is the load-bearing contract; the CLI is the I/O boundary. Tests cover all top-level export shapes (function declaration, default export, arrow + function expression assigned to `export const`, class methods including private/`#name`, constructors). Deterministic CI gate over a PR diff; pattern-catalogue conformance check                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Beck, _Extreme Programming Explained_, 1999 (CI as the constraint enforcer); Alexander et al., _A Pattern Language_, 1977 (catalogue indexed by artefact); rule #8 (pattern conformance — every artefact maps to a published pattern); rule #10 (deterministic enforcement)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Diff-based: every newly-added (status A) file under `novel/**`, root `*.md`, `setup.sh`, `distribution/**`, or `.github/workflows/**` must be mentioned by `vision.md` § "Pattern conformance index" (full path, package-prefix, or basename), unless the file's first ~20 lines carry `<!-- pattern: not-applicable — <reason> -->` (≥3-char reason). Test files (`*.test.ts`, `*.test.mjs`), fixture files, and node_modules are skipped. Modifications/renames/deletions are skipped — same grandfathering precedent as rule-1 / rule-3 / rule-4. Pure function `checkPatternIndex({ changedFiles, visionMdContent, optOuts })` is the load-bearing contract; CLI is the I/O boundary. `scripts/check-pr-self-grade.mjs` carry the load-bearing share. The Skill addresses ≤5 residual judgement-heavy concerns (A1 hypothesis vagueness, A5 conformance level mismatched to source — A2 pivot equals success was promoted to deterministic CI lint at row 39, A4 measurement output unchecked at row 40, A3 non-primary anchor at row 52, all retired from the Skill); growing past 5 requires either retiring one or shipping a deterministic linter (rule-#10 ratchet). **Deviation from Havelund-Goldberg 2008:** classical runtime verification produces deterministic verdicts; this Skill produces advisory verdicts only. The deterministic share of runtime verification lives in the linters above; this row covers the residual _judgement_ share that rule #10 explicitly carves out as "what cannot be deterministically checked is not a constitutional rule" — kept as an advisory companion, not an enforcement mechanism.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Closes the weekly–monthly layer of rule #9. Daily 09:00 UTC cron walks `experiment-store/*.jsonl`, finds the merge boundary, and re-runs the recorded `measurement` for any replay window whose boundary has elapsed. Pure function `replayExperiment(...)` is referentially transparent over `{ meta, record, priorReplays, currentValueStdout, now, ref, windowDays }`; the CLI is the I/O boundary. Verdict ladder: `validated` writes one line to `validated-learnings.md`; `regressed` (only after two consecutive pivot crossings — matches Risk mitigation) appends a `pivot-experiment-<id>` task to TASKS.md; `inconclusive` is the default. Corrupt JSONL surfaces as a warning, never crashes the run (rule #7).                                                                                                                                                          | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Closes the "Integration test (deferred)" clause in `distribution/README.md`. Exercises rows 1–4 of the failure-mode table empirically: SIGKILLs `minsky-tick-loop` and asserts respawn within 10 s (row 1), kills `minsky-budget-guard` and asserts permanent restart (row 3), verifies graceful stop does not respawn (row 2 weak form), runs a kill storm and asserts blast-radius containment (row 4). Linux mode bootstraps systemd `--user` units with a `loginctl enable-linger` + `dbus-run-session` fallback chain; macOS mode bootstraps LaunchAgents into `gui/$(id -u)`. Driver renders the templates' stub `run-*.sh` runners on the fly because the real tick-loop / budget-guard implementations land in follow-up tasks — restart-policy verification only depends on process presence. **Caveat:** GH-hosted Ubuntu runners' user-systemd plumbing is fragile; the driver exits 77 (skipped) if no user-bus is reachable, and the CI gate accepts `skipped` to preserve the merge path while still surfacing the signal when it runs. Pivot to a self-hosted runner is filed as `supervisor-integration-self-hosted-runner` in TASKS.md.                       | Pure decision function `checkPivotSuccessMargin({ success, pivot })` over the `@minsky/experiment-record`-parsed EXPERIMENT.yaml; CLI is the I/O boundary. Numeric extraction: scans each field for the first signed-numeric token (optional `-`/`+`, integer + optional fractional part). Decision table: both numeric → fail when `\|Δ\| / max(\|success\|, 1) < 1 %`; neither numeric and equal-after-trim/lower → fail (binary equality); neither numeric and differ → ok with advisory warning (long-tail prose stays advisory because the deterministic layer cannot read meaning); mixed → ok with advisory warning. Opt-out for legitimately-binary metrics: a top-level YAML comment line `# rule: ci-lint-pivot-success-margin: skip <reason>`. Pairs with `scripts/check-pivot-success-margin.test.mjs` (19 tests across the decision table). Ratchet: spec-monitor's `### A2.` heading is replaced by a tombstone `### A2 (retired …)` whose shape is intentionally outside the `check-skill-rule-cap` regex (rule #10's "remove the advisory rule when its deterministic counterpart ships" clause).                           | Pure function `checkMeasurementInspectsOutput(measurementCmd) => { ok, level: "fail" \| "warn" \| "pass", reason }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read EXPERIMENT.yaml, parse via `@minsky/experiment-record`, run the check, exit). Three-way verdict: `pass` (allowlist token present — `test`, `[`, `[[`, `jq -e`, `grep -q`/`-c`, `assert`, `vitest`, `pnpm test`/`typecheck`/`lint`, `@tasks-md/lint`, `markdownlint-cli2`, `gh run list ... --jq`, `node scripts/check-*.mjs`); `fail` (blacklist token present and no allowlist hit — `echo`, bare `true`, bare `curl`, bare `node`); `warn` (neither — residual judgement scope, exit 0 with stderr advisory). Word-boundary matching prevents `latest` from spuriously hitting `test`. Removed A4 from `novel/spec-monitor/SKILL.md` (the slot is reserved). `PromptOptimizer` interface, `StubPromptOptimizer`, `AnthropicPromptOptimizer`, and `selfTest()`; partial for `runABTest` (`sustainedGainAt7d` is always `false` in v0 — the Kohavi-Tang-Xu sustained-gain check ships in sub-task 3 against a verdict log spanning ≥7 d) and `structured<T>` (the schema is currently a system-prompt rider, not a tool-use response constraint — full conformance arrives when the Anthropic strategy adopts tool-use output enforcement) | Default Strategy is `AnthropicPromptOptimizer`; constructor accepts an injected `MessagesClient` so tests substitute a deterministic stub without an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. `selfTest()` returns `yellow` (graceful-degrade per rule #7) when no API key is set and no client was injected; `green` on a healthy injected client; `red` on injected-client error. Interface is the DSPy-fallback shape pre-registered in `research.md` § "DSPy fit" — the operationalisation of the rule-#2 invariant that vendor names not appear in business logic. Sub-tasks 2-4 (Monitor / Analyze / Plan / Execute / Knowledge phases of `mape-k-loop-v0`) consume this adapter.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Pure function `checkAnchorPrimarySource(anchor) => { ok, level: "fail" \| "warn" \| "pass", reason }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read EXPERIMENT.yaml, parse via `@minsky/experiment-record`, run the check, exit). Three-way verdict: `pass` (allowlist token present — italicised title `*…*`, `Ch. <n>`, `pp. <n>`, ISBN, DOI, `<VENUE> <YEAR>`, internal cross-ref `vision.md §` / `rule #<n>` / `spec-advisories/<date>.md`); `fail` (deny-list token present and no allowlist hit — `medium.com`, `*.substack.com`, `wikipedia.org`, `twitter.com`, `x.com`, `reddit.com`, `stackoverflow.com`, `chatgpt.com`, `claude.ai`, "ChatGPT said", "tweet by", "blog post"); `warn` (neither and anchor <25 chars — residual judgement scope, exit 0 with stderr advisory); long unrecognised prose passes silently. Allowlist wins over deny-list (a primary citation that contrasts against a Medium URL still passes). Opt-out: top-level YAML comment `# rule: ci-lint-anchor-primary-source: skip <reason ≥3 chars>`. Removed A3 from `novel/spec-monitor/SKILL.md` (the slot is reserved as a tombstone).
