| 54  | `novel/mape-k-loop/` (`@minsky/mape-k-loop` — pure `monitor(...)` + `analyze(...)` + `plan(...)` + `execute(...)` + `knowledge(...)` decision functions, `sustainedGain` + `oscillation` guards, and the assembled `tick(...)` cycle; all 4 sub-tasks of `mape-k-loop-v0` shipped)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | MAPE-K reference architecture (Monitor + Analyze + Plan + Execute + Knowledge phases) composed with Theory of Constraints (top constraint = max `violationCount × costEstimate`), pure-function decision module (Martin 2017 — every input is data, every output is data; the wrapping I/O is the user's), Kohavi-Tang-Xu sustained-gain window guard (default 7 d), Ries 2011 oscillation guard (default 10-iteration lookback), and a Helland 2007 immutable append-only log (`constraints.md`) as the Knowledge substrate                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Kephart & Chess, "The Vision of Autonomic Computing", _IEEE Computer_ 36(1) 2003 (the MAPE-K loop — Monitor / Analyze / Plan / Execute / Knowledge); Goldratt, _The Goal_, North River Press, 1984 (Theory of Constraints — the constraint is whatever currently bottlenecks throughput); Martin, _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (pure decision module + thin I/O boundary); Gregg, _Systems Performance_, Prentice Hall, 2014 (USE method — the per-rule aggregate-counter shape carries the _utilisation_ column; saturation + errors deferred to a follow-up); Kohavi, Tang, Xu, _Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments_, Cambridge UP 2020, Ch. 3 (the sustained-gain window — 7 d default — operationalised by `sustainedGain`); Ries, _The Lean Startup_, Crown Business 2011 (build-measure-learn — the oscillation guard is the "don't re-pivot to a previously-rejected variant" guardrail); Helland, "Life beyond Distributed Transactions", _CIDR_ 2007 (the append-only `constraints.md` Knowledge log); Munafò et al., "A Manifesto for Reproducible Science", _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (the rule-#9 calibration-drift amendment that the Knowledge phase emits when MAE exceeds threshold); rule #1 README opt-out (existing autonomic-manager runtimes — IBM Tivoli, OpenStack Heat, Kubernetes operator-sdk — rejected because their decision modules couple to a specific cluster substrate; Minsky's input shape is markdown + YAML + GH Actions JSON)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | full — M+A+P+E+K phases + 2 guards + assembled `tick(...)` shipped; the user-story-003 integration test passes against the assembled package end-to-end                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Pure functions `monitor(input)`, `analyze({ snapshot, costs? })`, `plan({ topConstraint, evidence, basePrompt })`, `execute({ variants, evalSet, optimizer, metric, history, now, sustainedGainWindowDays?, oscillationLookback? })`, `knowledge({ verdictLog, calibrationDriftThreshold?, topConstraintRuleId, executeDecision, executeReason, winnerVariantId, now })`, and `tick({ … })` are referentially transparent — no I/O, no shared state, clocks injected via `now`. `tick` runs M → A → P → E → K and threads an optional `emit` seam so the wrapping CLI can carry OTEL spans (`mape.<phase>.<verb>`) without coupling the pure functions to `@minsky/observability`. `knowledge` writes a markdown block to append to `constraints.md` (every tick is logged — even no-ops; Helland 2007), and _conditionally_ a `researchMdAmendmentProposal` text when the verdict-log MAE between predicted and observed Δ exceeds the `calibrationDriftThreshold` (default 50 % per Munafò et al. 2017 — pre-registration discipline). `monitor` aggregates parsed `CiRun` / `Advisory` / `ExperimentRecord` rows into a per-rule `HealthSnapshot`; corrupt rows are dropped with a `warnings` entry (graceful-degrade per rule #7) instead of throwing. `analyze` ranks rules by `violationCount × costEstimate(ruleId)`; ties are broken alphabetically by `ruleId` so output is deterministic across runs (matching the prompt-optimizer `pickWinner` tie-break in row 53). `plan` proposes ≤3 variants from a fixed v0 mutation catalogue (`enumerate-failure-modes`, `direct-answer`, `tighten-scope`); empty `ruleId` throws (let-it-crash on caller contract violation). `execute` runs the A/B via the `PromptOptimizer` adapter (row 53), then applies the two guards: `sustainedGain` returns `{ ok: false }` for cold starts, mid-window swaps, or windows shorter than the configured `windowDays`; `oscillation` blocks re-proposal of any variant that was `rejected` or `abstain`-ed within the last `lookbackIterations`. The `costs` argument is the seam where the `costEstimate` schedule sources per-rule severity weights from vision.md § "Cost schedule" (see that section for the v0 default — `rule-9 = 100`, `rule-7 = 50`, `rule-typo = 1`). `parseCostSchedule(visionMdContent)` (`novel/mape-k-loop/src/cost-schedule.ts`) is the upstream parser; the CLI wrapper threads its output into `tick({ ..., costs })` which forwards it to `analyze`. `costEstimate` is now full-conformance — the schedule is configurable, sourced from a literature-anchored constant in source per rule #4, and falls back to `DEFAULT_RULE_COST = 1` for any rule absent from the table (rule #7 graceful-degrade). Severity buckets (`low` / `medium` / `high`) thresholded at product ≥3 / ≥8 — conservative v0 cutoffs.                               | Pure function `checkConstraintsMdSize({ content, capEntries }) => { ok, count, reason? }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read `novel/mape-k-loop/constraints.md`, run the check, exit). Counts lines matching `^## YYYY-MM-DD` (strict ISO-8601 date match — backticked prose mentions of the heading shape in the README preamble are not counted). Boundary semantics: the cap is _inclusive_ — exactly 200 entries passes (Beyer SRE 2016 Ch. 3 — "you have used X % of your budget" is not a violation until X _exceeds_ it; matches `check-mape-k-budget-cap`'s precedent). `capEntries` defaults to 200 (the README prose anchor); the constant is locked by a paired test so a silent edit surfaces loudly. Dormant state (rule #7 — graceful degrade): missing `constraints.md` exits 0 with a stderr advisory and re-arms the moment the file lands — same precedent as `check-skill-rule-cap.mjs`'s retired-Skill terminal state. Pivot (rule #9): if 200 proves arbitrary (the file is still readable at 400), raise the constant rather than removing the lint — removing it would re-open the unbounded-growth failure mode rule-#6 closes mechanically. Dormant state (rule #7 — graceful degrade): missing `config/mape-k.json` exits 0 with a stderr advisory and re-arms the moment the artefact lands — same precedent as `check-skill-rule-cap.mjs`'s retired-Skill terminal state. Pivot (rule #9): if the 5.7 % cap moves to a per-tier adaptive threshold (per `mape-k-loop`'s monthly self-calibration, research.md § "MAPE-K cadence"), retire this lint and replace with one that reads the calibrated value from the `mape-k-loop` Knowledge log. backstop's anchor); Ries, _The Lean Startup_, Crown Business 2011 (pivot-or-persevere — the cadence-pivot threshold's anchor); Beck, _Extreme Programming Explained_, 1999 (CI as the constraint enforcer)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Each lint is a pure decision function over `{ config, expected }` returning `{ ok, reason? }`; CLI is the I/O boundary (read `config/<file>.json`, run the check, exit). Tick-loop reads `config/tick-loop.json`; the three MAPE-K-related lints all read `config/mape-k.json` (separate jobs, narrow assertions) — the cadence-pivot threshold colocates here per its TASKS.md "Files" brief, not in a separate `config/cadence.json`, because it is a property of the MAPE-K cadence. Each accepts two field-name shapes (e.g., `backoff_schedule` OR `backoff_schedule_seconds`; `tick_iteration_backstop` OR `tick_iteration_backstop_ticks`; `watchdog_hours` OR `watchdog_period_hours`; `cadence_pivot_threshold_pct` OR `cadence_pivot_spend_fraction`) so the artefact author can pick whichever shape reads best — the strict equality check (or fraction-percent normalisation, for the cadence-pivot lint) is the gate, not the field name. Default expected values are locked by paired tests so a silent edit to a constant surfaces loudly (rule-#10 ratchet — drift requires a Pivot, not a one-line PR). Dormant state (rule #7 — graceful degrade): missing `config/<file>.json` exits 0 with a stderr advisory and re-arms the moment the artefact lands — same precedent as `check-mape-k-budget-cap.mjs` and `check-skill-rule-cap.mjs`'s retired-Skill terminal state. Pivot (rule #9, per-lint): if the prose anchor itself moves before the corresponding config artefact lands (e.g., the fixed `5s → 30s → 5min` ladder is replaced by exponential backoff, or the 12 h watchdog is replaced by adaptive cadence per `mape-k-loop`'s monthly self-calibration extending T to 18 h), retire that specific lint and write a new one against the replacement anchor. (`TokenExtractor.extract_tokens` summing `input_tokens + output_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens`), `claude_monitor/data/analyzer.py` (5h `SessionBlock` rounding rule), `claude_monitor/core/plans.py` (`PLAN_LIMITS = { pro: 19_000, max5: 88_000, max20: 220_000, custom: 44_000 }`)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Constructor opts: `{ configDir: string, now?: () => Date, plan?: 'pro' \| 'max5' \| 'max20' \| 'custom' }`; `now` defaults to `() => new Date()`, `plan` defaults to `'max5'` (the most common Anthropic tier). `PLAN_CAPS` is exported as a named constant (rule #4 — every magic number named) and locked by paired tests. `snapshot()` rglobs `<configDir>/projects/**/*.jsonl`, stream-parses line-by-line with `JSON.parse` (skip blanks; tolerate malformed JSON per rule #7 graceful-degrade), filters to entries with `message.usage` and positive token sum, dedups by `(message.id, requestId)` (Maciek's rule when the same message appears in two files — resumed sessions write a fresh JSONL), groups sorted entries into 5h SessionBlocks (`block.start = floor(first-entry.ts, hour)` UTC; new block when `entry.ts >= block.end_time` OR `gap-since-last-entry >= 5h`), picks the active block (`now ∈ [start, end)`), sums `input + output + cache_creation + cache_read`, returns `{ tokensRemainingInWindow: max(0, planCap − used), windowSizeTokens: planCap, secondsUntilWindowReset, weeklyHeadroomFraction: 0, observedAt }`. Cold-start path (missing `configDir`, no JSONL files, all-malformed) returns the full plan cap as remaining. **Path correction (rule #9 pivot fired)**: prior brief said `~/.config/claude/`; that env-var path (`CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`) only governs Maciek's _own_ settings cache. The actual data source is `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/<session>.jsonl` — Claude Code writes one JSONL file per session (filename = session UUID), grouped under one directory per cwd (directory name = cwd path with `/` → `-`). 5 committed synthetic fixtures (`single-block`, `two-blocks`, `empty`, `malformed`, `dedup`) under `novel/adapters/token-monitor/test/fixtures/<scenario>/projects/-fixture-cwd/`; each scenario carries a sibling `expected.json` documenting the expected snapshot. **No real `~/.claude/projects/` data is committed.**                         | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Pure functions `roundTripOmcTask(taskJson) => { ok, diff? }`, `findOmcTaskFiles(omcCheckout) => string[]`, and `parseOmcCheckoutFlag(argv) => string \| null` are referentially transparent — no I/O, no shared state. The CLI (`main()`) is the I/O boundary: parses `--omc-checkout=<path>`, walks `<path>/.omc/state/team/<teamName>/tasks/*.json`, runs `roundTripOmcTask` on each. Whitespace normalisation strips per-line trailing whitespace and collapses trailing newlines so the `JSON.stringify-indent-2` output is comparable to the OMC on-disk bytes; leading whitespace (indentation) is _not_ stripped — a non-canonical indent (e.g., 4-space) is a structural fail (the load-bearing pivot trigger). Dormant state (rule #7): missing `--omc-checkout=` OR `<path>/.omc/state/team/` not a directory → exit 0 with stderr advisory; same precedent as `check-mape-k-budget-cap.mjs`'s dormant-config short-circuit. Test coverage (12 cases in `scripts/omc-roundtrip.test.mjs`): synthetic task round-trips cleanly, whitespace-only trailing diff is OK, non-canonical indent fails with a divergence diff, invalid JSON fails with parse-error diff, non-object top-level fails with shape-error diff, non-string input fails fast (rule-#6 let-it-crash on caller contract violation), `findOmcTaskFiles` walks multi-team directories + ignores non-JSON neighbours + returns empty on missing dir, `parseOmcCheckoutFlag` handles absent / present / empty-value cases. Synthetic fixtures only — no real `~/.claude/` or third-party data committed. | 62  | `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/` (`@minsky/omc-tasksmd-bridge` v0 — read-only OMC team-task JSON → tasks.md projection: pure `mapOmcToTasksMd(task)` mapper + pure `syncOmcToTasksMd({ omcTasks, existingTasksMd, mode: 'replace-section' })` section-replace + thin `OmcReader.list({ repoRoot, teamName? })` filesystem walker; closes `omc-tasksmd-bridge-v0`'s "Round-trip preserves all task fields" Acceptance through the documented lossy projection — the v0 surface field-mapping is in `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/README.md` § "Lossy projection") + `novel/bridges/README.md` (namespace home, intentionally separate from `novel/adapters/` because a _bridge_ is a peer-to-peer format pair we own, not an external dep behind an interface)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Read-only adapter bridge between two task-list formats — Helland 2007 (eventual consistency, only the read direction in v0 because OMC's optimistic-concurrency `version` field needs a CRDT story before reverse-sync is safe — filed as `omc-tasksmd-bridge-v1-watcher` in TASKS.md); Hewitt 1973 actor model (TASKS.md is the message store, each emitted block is one message addressed to whoever picks it next); pure-function projection + section-replace idempotency (Knuth 1984 — referential transparency means re-running yields byte-equal output, the load-bearing property for a bridge that may run on a v1+ watch loop); the read-side I/O boundary is a single `fs/promises.readdir` + `readFile` pair (Martin 2017 _Clean Architecture_ — I/O at the edge, mapper + sync are pure)                                                               | Helland, P., "Life beyond Distributed Transactions: an Apostate's Opinion", _CIDR_ 2007 (the load-bearing pattern: a bridge between two stores converges through eventual consistency; v0 ships only the read direction because that's the safe half until the write-side has a CRDT story); Hewitt, Bishop, Steiger, "A Universal Modular Actor Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", _IJCAI_ 1973 (TASKS.md as the message store; each emitted block is one message); Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, _Design Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Adapter / Bridge — the namespace-level pattern, distinct from rule #2's _dependency_ adapters); Aho, Sethi, Ullman, _Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools_, Addison-Wesley, 1986 (parser shape — `JSON.parse` is the only parser; the format is the round-trip-parseable verdict from `scripts/omc-roundtrip.mjs` per row 61); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (I/O at the edge — `OmcReader.list` is the only filesystem touch; mapper + sync are pure); Munafò et al., _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the lossy-projection pivot was declared in `EXPERIMENT.yaml` _before_ code so a `metadata: { … }` field that doesn't fit the documented projection cannot retroactively redefine "lossless"; v0 ships _with_ the lossy note rather than expanding scope); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; OMC's task store is exactly that, behind `OmcReader.list`); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; the v1+ `merge-by-id` mode throws with a `v1+` message so the caller decides next step rather than the bridge silently doing the wrong thing); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — graceful-degrade on missing `.omc/state/team/`, on malformed JSON, and on unknown `teamName` filter); research.md § "OMC handoff persistence" (the parseable verdict that unblocked v0); TASKS.md `omc-tasksmd-bridge-v1-watcher` (the next-tier follow-up: chokidar / `fs.watch` + reverse-sync once the CRDT story lands)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | **Lossy-projection v0 (declared deviation, pre-registered).** v0 surfaces the documented subset — `id`, `subject`, `description`, `status`, `owner ?? claim.owner`, `blocked_by` (comma-space-joined), `created_at`, `version` — and drops `claim.token`, `claim.leased_until`, `blocks`, `depends_on`, `result`, `error`, `requires_code_change`, `metadata`. The drop is _intentional_ (the OMC fields are out of v0 scope; surfacing them in tasks.md would require either widening the tasks.md spec or shipping a second projection mode), and is the load-bearing reason conformance is `partial` rather than `full`. The pivot was pre-declared in `EXPERIMENT.yaml`'s `pivot:` block — "if any field can't be losslessly mapped, document the lossy projection in `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/README.md` and ship anyway with a 'lossy v0' note" — so the deviation cannot be retroactively explained away (Munafò 2017 pre-registration). 17 paired tests under `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/src/{reader,mapper,sync}.test.ts` cover: cold-start (missing `.omc/state/team/`), malformed JSON skip with stderr advisory, unknown `teamName` filter, multi-team walk (alphabetical), minimum-fields and full-record mapping, `[x]` vs `[ ]` checkbox, `owner ?? claim.owner` precedence, idempotent re-run (byte-equal output), preservation of non-OMC sections, replace-in-place over a stale section (no duplicates), empty-list section, and the `merge-by-id` v1+ throw. Synthetic fixtures only under `novel/bridges/omc-tasksmd/test/fixtures/` — `task-001.json` (full record with claim + metadata), `task-002.json` (completed status + completed*at), `malformed.json` (intentionally invalid JSON for the rule-#7 graceful-skip chaos test, biome-excluded so the linter doesn't try to parse it), and `other-team/task-otr-001.json` (multi-team walk fixture). **No real OMC state from a live install is committed.** Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8). All exported functions/methods carry `@otel bridges.omc-tasksmd.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4; helper / type-guard / coercion helpers are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6, with three `// rule-6: handled-locally — graceful-skip …` opt-outs at the file-read / JSON-parse / readDir boundaries. The reader is the \_only* I/O boundary; mapper + sync are pure (no clock dependency, no shared state). v1+ direction (`omc-tasksmd-bridge-v1-watcher`, P3 in TASKS.md): chokidar / `fs.watch` reverse-sync after a CRDT story for OMC's `version` field. full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No binary bundled in-tree** — `install-openobserve.sh` downloads OpenObserve `v0.80.1` (pinned) from the upstream GitHub release URL at install time. Platform detection covers `darwin-arm64` / `darwin-amd64` / `linux-amd64` / `linux-arm64`; unsupported platform exits non-zero with a clear error. Binary lands at `${HOME}/.local/bin/openobserve`; data dir at `${HOME}/.openobserve/data`. The launchd plist + systemd unit both use `${HOME}` as their only placeholder (consistent with `distribution/launchd/`'s `${MINSKY_HOME}` precedent — but OpenObserve's daemon doesn't need `MINSKY_HOME` because it's a standalone receiver). **Read-only Strategy by construction** — `queryOpenObservePromql` only issues HTTP GETs against `/api/<org>/prometheus/api/v1/query`; there is no POST / PUT / DELETE code path in the dashboard side. The OTLP write side is handled by `@minsky/observability` via the official OTLP HTTP exporters, never by the dashboard. **18 paired Strategy tests** in `novel/dashboard-web/test/strategy.test.ts` cover: snapshot lookup (existing — 3), constant lookup (existing — 1), Prometheus instant-query parser (5: scalar, vector, error status, malformed JSON, empty vector), `queryOpenObservePromql` (5: GET-shape, basic auth, non-2xx, network failure, trailing-slash normalisation), `fetchOpenObserveSnapshot` (3: fan-out, partial failure, format hook), `openObserveGetValue` end-to-end (1: async pre-fetch + sync render-time lookup). **9 OTEL adapter tests** in `novel/adapters/observability/src/otel.test.ts` cover: existing self-test (5), `endpoint` opt flow (1) + trailing-slash normalisation (1) + undefined-endpoint env-fallback (1) + constructor-doesn't-throw with endpoint (1). **Integration test gated** on `OPENOBSERVE_INTEGRATION=1` env var — skipped in CI by default; runs locally when the user has installed OpenObserve. **Install script syntax-validated** via `bash -n distribution/install-openobserve.sh` (the load-bearing CI gate per rule #10 — no actual install runs in CI). All exported functions carry `@otel <verb>` JSDoc per rule #4; pure helpers (`stripTrailingSlash`, `btoaCompat`, `parsePromqlSuccessData`, `extractScalarValue`, `extractVectorFirstValue`, `resolveOtlpEndpoints`) are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (the parser is split into 4 helper functions). Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6, with two `// rule-6: handled-locally — graceful-degrade …` opt-outs at the network and JSON-parse boundaries. **Resilience-scout follow-ups (≤2 per the scout cap)**: (a) the metric _names_ in `OPENOBSERVE_PROMQL_BY_METRIC_ID` (`token_count`, `supervisor_restart_to_claim_latency_seconds`, `http_get_total{path="/watch.json"}`, `claude_code_api_errors_total`) are the exact strings vision.md publishes — if `@minsky/observability` ever emits under different names, the mismatch is filed as `otel-emission-name-audit` (P3 follow-up); (b) the OpenObserve binary URL pattern (`releases/download/${VERSION}/openobserve-${VERSION}-${target}.tar.gz`) may change — if the quarterly review fires the pivot, fall back to VictoriaMetrics triad per `research.md` PR #43 (the runner-up, MetricsQL is a near-superset of PromQL — migration cost is low).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 73  | `experiments/` directory + `scripts/check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` (`experiments-directory-migration` — moves the rule-#9 pre-registration substrate from a singleton `EXPERIMENT.yaml` at the repo root to plural `experiments/<id>.yaml` files, one per pre-registered experiment; backwards-compatible at the per-file CLI boundary — every lint still accepts an explicit path; new directory-walker default for the three rule-#9 lints; a one-way ratchet `check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` asserts the singleton stays gone after the migration; CI workflows `ci.yml` + `experiment.yml` updated to walk the directory)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Pre-registration record (Munafò et al. 2017 — one record per pre-registration, the singular shape AsPredicted enforces) + parametric path root (Hewitt 1973 — the directory is the substrate; making the root parametric via the lint's CLI argument lets the same lint run against minsky's own `experiments/` and against a host's `${MINSKY_HOST_ROOT}/.minsky/experiments/` once `host-root-resolver-prep` lands) + one-way ratchet (rule #10 — when an advisory or transitional state is replaced by a new structural answer, the old state cannot quietly come back); concurrent-experiments support (Fagerholm et al. RCoSE 2014 — the per-change runner must support multiple A/B tests in flight)                                                                                                                                                          | Munafò, M. R., Nosek, B. A., Bishop, D. V. M., et al., "A manifesto for reproducible science", _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (the singular-record-per-pre-registration shape — `experiments/<id>.yaml` matches the AsPredicted source pattern; the singleton was a Minsky-coined deviation this migration retires); Fagerholm, F., Guinea, A. S., Mäenpää, H., Münch, J., "Building Blocks for Continuous Experimentation", _RCoSE_ 2014 (the per-change experiment runner is the first building block — and it must support plural concurrent experiments per the paper's "multiple A/B tests in flight" design); Beck, K., _Extreme Programming Explained_, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Ch. 17 (CI as the constraint enforcer — the directory-walker is the smallest deterministic substrate that supports plural experiments); Hewitt, C., "A Universal Modular ACTOR Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", _IJCAI_ 1973 (parametric path root — the substrate boundary is the actor's interface; making the experiments root parametric is the precondition for cross-repo support landing in `host-root-resolver-prep`); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent: the data layer at `experiment-store/<id>.jsonl` is already plural; the migration matches the pre-registration layer to it, not building a new substrate); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — the migration itself is pre-registered in `experiments/experiments-directory-migration.yaml`; the eat-own-dogfood test); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the singleton-ratchet `check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` is the structural one-way guarantee that the singleton stays gone); TASKS.md `experiments-directory-migration` (the parent task this row closes) + `cross-repo-runner-v0` / `host-root-resolver-prep` (the consumers — both are now unblocked because the per-task substrate is plural); user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md (the cross-repo runner's umbrella experiment record graduates to `experiments/006-runner-on-any-repo.yaml` per this migration).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Backward-compatible at the file CLI; new directory default at the no-arg CLI.** The three rule-#9 lints (`check-anchor-primary-source.mjs`, `check-measurement-inspects-output.mjs`, `check-pivot-success-margin.mjs`) each export a new `mainDirectory(directoryPath)` that walks `experiments/*.yaml` (and `*.yml`) sorted alphabetically, runs the per-file `main(file)` on each, and aggregates exit codes (max wins — any single non-zero fails the run). The CLI default switches from `EXPERIMENT.yaml` to `experiments/`; explicit-path invocations (`node scripts/check-X.mjs path/to/file.yaml`) still work for explicit single-file linting. The new ratchet `scripts/check-no-singleton-experiment.mjs` is a pure check (no LLM, no opt-out): exits 1 if `EXPERIMENT.yaml` exists at the repo root, 0 otherwise. The CI workflow `ci.yml` adds a `no-singleton-experiment` job following the `skill-rule-cap` pattern (runs on every event; required check via `needs:`); existing lint job invocations drop the explicit `EXPERIMENT.yaml` arg so they default to walking the directory. The `experiment.yml` workflow's `gate` and `record` jobs now loop over `experiments/*.yaml` (and a defensive `EXPERIMENT.yaml` if the singleton sneaks back, surfacing the regression at the gate before the ratchet). 18 paired tests ship with the migration: 5 walker tests per lint × 3 lints (15) plus 3 ratchet tests = 18 new vitest cases on top of the pre-existing suites. The migrated `experiments/readme-global-install-claim-fix-2026-05-04.yaml` is the first plural file; `experiments/experiments-directory-migration.yaml` is the migration's own pre-registration (the eat-own-dogfood test). The pivot path — per-experiment-id PR-diff scope when a single bad file gates ≥3 unrelated PRs over 14 days — is recorded on the migration's own EXPERIMENT.yaml so the walker's blast-radius failure mode has a structural escape valve. The `novel/experiment-record/spec.md` § "File location" is rewritten to document the plural shape; the singleton mention is replaced. Cross-repo runs (cross-repo-runner-v0, blocked-by this) consume this substrate as `${MINSKY_HOST_ROOT}/.minsky/experiments/<id>.yaml` once `host-root-resolver-prep` parametrises the lint's root. | 74  | `scripts/lib/host-root.mjs` + `scripts/lib/host-root.test.mjs` + parametric `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` wiring in `scripts/check-anchor-primary-source.mjs` + `scripts/check-measurement-inspects-output.mjs` + `scripts/check-pivot-success-margin.mjs` (`host-root-resolver-prep` — preparation PR per rule #9 that introduces `getHostRoot(repoRoot)` reading `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` from the environment; the three rule-#9 substrate lints now resolve their `experiments/` root through the helper; behaviour-preserving for minsky-on-itself — the helper returns `repoRoot` when the env var is unset; cross-repo invocations point at a host's `.minsky/` sidecar where the layout mirrors the minsky repo root)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Parametric path root (Hewitt 1973 — the substrate boundary is the actor's interface; making the experiments root parametric via an env var lets the same lint live in many filesystems) + dependency injection at the edge (Martin 2017 — the helper is a one-line override; consumers call once at module init; the rest of each script is unchanged) + preparation-PR pattern (rule #9 — when the metric source needs to exist before the artefact that depends on it can be built, ship the substrate first)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Hewitt, C., "A Universal Modular ACTOR Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", _IJCAI_ 1973 (the substrate boundary is the actor's interface; making it parametric is the precondition for cross-repo lint reuse); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (DI at the edge — `getHostRoot` is the substrate-root injection point, the only env-var read in the lint); Helland, P., "Life beyond Distributed Transactions: an Apostate's Opinion", _CIDR_ 2007 (the substrate-locality boundary defines the consistency boundary; making it explicit is the precondition for cross-system semantics); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every external dep through an interface; here the "external dep" is the _filesystem root_, made explicit via the helper); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — preparation-PR pattern: the helper lands before the cross-repo runner that consumes it, so the runner can ship as a _small_ PR rather than co-shipping the substrate refactor); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the helper is a pure function over `process.env.MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` + the supplied `repoRoot`, no LLM in the chain); user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md (the umbrella story this helper enables); docs/cross-repo-portability.md (the operative classification — the three rule-#9 lints port into the cross-repo-portable bucket via this helper); rows 71 + 73 (the experiments-directory-migration this helper extends — same substrate, parametric root); TASKS.md `host-root-resolver-prep` (the parent task this row closes) + `minsky-sidecar-bootstrap` / `cross-repo-runner-v0` (the consumers — both unblocked once this row ships).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Behaviour-preserving for minsky-on-itself; new cross-repo path is opt-in via env.** `getHostRoot(repoRoot, envOverride?)` reads `process.env.MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` (or the optional injected env for tests). Returns the env value when set + non-empty (resolved to absolute), otherwise the supplied `repoRoot` (also resolved). 8 paired tests in `scripts/lib/host-root.test.mjs` cover: unset → repo, empty → repo, set → override, relative override resolution, relative repo fallback resolution, env wins over repo, default `process.env` lookup, defensive type-guard for malformed envs. The three rule-#9 substrate lints (`anchor-primary-source` / `measurement-inspects-output` / `pivot-success-margin`) each replace their `resolve(REPO_ROOT, "experiments")` with `resolve(getHostRoot(REPO_ROOT), "experiments")`. Smoke-tested cross-repo: `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT=/tmp/<fixture> node scripts/check-X.mjs` walks the fixture's `experiments/` and exits 0 when the records are valid; same shell command with the env var unset walks minsky's own `experiments/` (the existing two records). 372 vitest cases pass (8 new + the existing 364). Typecheck clean. Biome clean. The helper is the smallest possible carve-out — three lines of substantive logic — so the consumer-side change in each lint is one import + one constant rebinding. The remaining 9 rule lints (rule-1, rule-2, rule-3, rule-4, rule-5, rule-7, pattern-index, skill-rule-cap, mape-k-\* family) deliberately do NOT take the env var in this PR — they ship cross-repo only when the host's source taxonomy enters the constitutional gate (per `docs/cross-repo-portability.md`'s classification, that's deferred to a follow-up step or skipped by-design for rule-1). The deferred lints are not blockers for the cross-repo runner v0 because their substrate is host-source-tree, not host-experiments. Pivot path: if the env var proves brittle in a host repo (e.g., the operator's shell strips env vars between minsky CLI invocation and child-process spawn), pivot to a CLI flag (`--host-root <path>`) on each lint as a v1 follow-up; the helper's signature already accepts an `envOverride` parameter, so the CLI wrapper can synthesize an env from the flag without touching the helper. | 75  | `novel/sidecar-bootstrap/` (`@minsky/sidecar-bootstrap` v0 — `minsky bootstrap <host-dir>` CLI that materialises a per-host gitignored `.minsky/` sidecar so the cross-repo-runner can govern any host repo; pure planner / inferer / diagnoser / schema in `src/` + thin CLI executor in `bin/minsky-bootstrap.mjs`; idempotent re-runs, `--doctor` self-test with green/yellow/red Avizienis lattice, `--repair` mode; mkdir-based concurrency lock at `.minsky/.bootstrap.lock.d` exits 75 on race)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Command pattern (Gamma 1994 — actions are inspectable data the executor walks) + pure-function-with-I/O-at-edge (Martin 2017 — planner / inferer / doctor are referentially transparent; CLI is the only side-effecting layer) + status lattice (Avizienis et al., _IEEE TDSC_ 1 (1), 2004 — `aggregateStatus` red dominates yellow dominates green) + idempotent-bootstrap-via-lock (Armstrong 2007 — `setup.sh`'s mkdir-based lock idiom; second invocation exits EX_TEMPFAIL)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, _Design Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Command pattern — `BootstrapAction` is a discriminated union the executor switches on; testable as data without I/O); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (DI at the edge — the `dist/` exports are pure; the CLI binds to `node:fs` once at the top level); Avizienis, A., Laprie, J.-C., Randell, B., Landwehr, C., "Basic Concepts and Taxonomy of Dependable and Secure Computing", _IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing_ 1 (1), 2004 (the green/yellow/red lattice of `diagnose()` mirrors the existing `@minsky/adapter-types` `aggregateStatus()`; red dominates yellow dominates green); Armstrong, J., _Programming Erlang_, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (idempotent supervisor restart — the bootstrap is the host's "start" verb; second invocation must be a no-op, third with `--repair` must restore invariants; the mkdir-based lock at `.bootstrap.lock.d` mirrors `setup.sh`'s atomic-claim idiom and surfaces concurrent races as EX*TEMPFAIL=75 per BSD sysexits.h); Hunt & Thomas, \_The Pragmatic Programmer*, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Tip 8 ("Make It Easy to Reuse" — inference reads existing host signals like `package.json` + `.git/config` rather than re-eliciting); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent: the bootstrap reuses the host's signals, the YAML is hand-rendered to avoid a yaml dep, the CLI reuses the existing `setup.sh` lock idiom); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every external dep through an interface; the host's filesystem layout IS the dep, made explicit via the `RepoConfig` schema); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash AT the boundary; the planner returns the action list, the executor handles per-action errors with operator-actionable messages, no silent failures); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — failure-mode discipline; the README's chaos table has 7 rows including bad-input / host-not-fully-onboarded / concurrency / host-config-drift / filesystem-permission / upstream-malformed / external-cleanup); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered pivot for the global-ignore mechanism: if `~/.config/git/ignore` proves unwritable on enough hosts, fall back to per-clone `.git/info/exclude`; this pivot is wired into the `RepoConfig.ignore_mechanism` field's three-value enum); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the bootstrap's behaviour is fully deterministic given typed inputs and a host filesystem state); user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md (the umbrella story this package enables); rows 71 (experiments-directory-migration substrate) + 74 (host-root-resolver — the env var the bootstrap's sidecar plugs into); TASKS.md `minsky-sidecar-bootstrap` (the parent task this row closes) + `cross-repo-runner-v0` (the immediate downstream consumer that reads `.minsky/repo.yaml`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No real host filesystem in CI; all I/O at the CLI edge.** 51 paired vitest cases across `src/{schema,inference,plan,doctor}.test.ts` cover: schema (12 tests — happy path × 6, validation failures × 6 covering missing required fields, empty strings, non-string types, invalid enums); inference (15 tests — defaults, git-remote URL parsing for SSH / HTTPS / no-suffix / malformed forms, package.json detection of yarn / pnpm / npm with vs without `lint` script, workspaces detection, default-branch fallback); planner (14 tests — fresh-host all-six-actions emission, idempotent re-run produces only the log-info action, partial-state surfaces only missing artefacts, `ignore_mechanism: per-clone-exclude` and `none` skip the append-to-ignore action, YAML rendering covers all required fields including `null` and empty-overrides cases, round-trip-ability via structural inspection); doctor (10 tests — all-green, each red path individually, yellow on missing global-ignore, red-dominates-yellow aggregation, yellow-dominates-green aggregation, all-green aggregation). Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8). All exported functions carry `@otel sidecar-bootstrap.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`parse-repo-config`, `infer-repo-config`, `plan-bootstrap`, `render-repo-yaml`, `diagnose`); helpers (`requireString`, `inferHostRepo`, `inferCommitFormat`, `inferPreCommitCommand`, `inferTicketFormat`, `inferHostPackagesPath`, `quote`) are JSDoc-internal-only. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 — the planner has zero try/catch; the executor's `applyAction` switches on the action `kind` and lets per-action errors bubble to the CLI's top-level catch. The CLI's `acquireLock` exits 75 on EEXIST per Erlang/OTP idempotent-supervisor-restart discipline. End-to-end smoke-tested 2026-05-04 against a tmpdir host with a synthetic `package.json` + git remote: bootstrap inferred `host_repo: "test-owner/test-repo"` from `git@github.com:test-owner/test-repo.git`, `pre_commit_command: "yarn lint"` from `packageManager: yarn@4.0.0` + a `lint` script, wrote 5 sidecar artefacts + 1 global-ignore entry, doctor returned GREEN, idempotent re-run was a no-op (only the log-info action). The `--repair` mode currently re-runs the planner (the planner skips already-present artefacts so the result is identical to a normal run); a future enhancement could force-overwrite a corrupted `repo.yaml`, but v0 keeps it identical for simplicity.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | **No real subprocess in CI; v0 ships dry-run as the safe default.** 43 paired vitest cases across 5 files: `task-finder.test.ts` (14 — TASKS.md parsing for sections / ID / tags / details / rule-#9 fields; exact-ID match; case-sensitive ID lookup; title-substring matching for ticket-key form like `AIFN-840`; not-found reporting with available IDs); `<host>/.minsky/experiments/<id>.yaml`; `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` / `MINSKY_TASK_ID` / `MINSKY_BRANCH_NAME` env vars; system-prompt overlay references vision.md path + `Hypothesis self-grade` requirement + pre_commit_command branch); `iteration-record.test.ts` (4 — JSONL render shape with trailing newline + valid JSON + null pr_url + 7 verdict variants); `repo-config-loader.test.ts` (3 — flat-YAML parser handling sidecar-bootstrap's render shape including nested-map `lint_substrate_overrides` + comments + the `{}` empty-map sentinel; loadRepoConfig end-to-end happy + missing-required-field paths). Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (every function ≤10 from first writes — the planner / synthesiser / loader were all designed as straight-line code; no helper extractions needed). All exported functions carry `@otel cross-repo-runner.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`load-repo-config`, `parse-flat-yaml`, `find-task`, `parse-tasks-md`, `synthesise-experiment-yaml`, `build-spawn-plan`, `render-iteration-record`). End-to-end smoke-tested 2026-05-04 against a tmpdir host: bootstrap created `.minsky/repo.yaml`; `minsky run aifn-840-slash-command-labels --host <tmpdir>` parsed the task, synthesised the EXPERIMENT.yaml at `.minsky/experiments/aifn-840-slash-command-labels.yaml` (with all 5 rule-#9 fields), wrote the runner plan to stdout (taskId / branchName / env / system-prompt overlay / brief), appended the `verdict: planned` iteration record to `.minsky/experiment-store/cross-repo/aifn-840-slash-command-labels.jsonl`. A separate smoke against a TASKS.md row missing all 5 rule-#9 fields exited 1 with `rule-9 violation: task ... is missing required field(s): Hypothesis, Success, Pivot, Measurement, Anchor` and `Rule #9 is iron — no exemption` — exactly the rule-#9 iron-rule discipline the synthesiser enforces. Conformance is `partial` (not `full`) because v0's `--live` flag is a placeholder — it writes the EXPERIMENT.yaml + plan but doesn't yet spawn Claude Code; the spawn-wiring (importing `@minsky/tick-loop` + `@minsky/budget-guard` and invoking them from the bin) ships in v1 to keep v0 small and reviewable. The seam is already in the system-prompt overlay + brief that the bin produces; v1 just adds the spawn call. The `cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-integration-test` step ships AIFN-840 against a real iep-capabilities host — that's the v0 acceptance for the umbrella user-story-006 metric                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **9 paired vitest cases run against tmpdir hosts via the real `minsky-bootstrap.mjs` + `minsky-run.mjs` CLIs.** Each test creates a tmpdir, initialises it as a git repo with a synthetic remote URL (`git@github.com:test-org/test-iep-capabilities.git`) + a `package.json` with `lint` script + `packageManager: "yarn@4.0.0"`, writes an AIFN-840-shaped `TASKS.md` row, runs the bootstrap CLI under an isolated `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` so the test's global-ignore writes don't pollute the operator's real config, runs the runner CLI, asserts the resulting filesystem state. The 9 cases cover: (1) bootstrap creates `.minsky/repo.yaml` + `.minsky/experiments/` + `.minsky/vision.md` symlink with inferred values (`host_repo: "test-org/test-iep-capabilities"`, `pre_commit_command: "yarn lint"`); (2) runner dry-run synthesises the EXPERIMENT.yaml at `.minsky/experiments/<id>.yaml` with all 5 rule-#9 fields rendered correctly (id, hypothesis, success, pivot, measurement, anchor); (3) runner appends iteration record to `.minsky/experiment-store/cross-repo/<id>.jsonl` with `verdict: planned`, `host_repo`, `branch: feat/<task-id>`, JSON-parseable shape; (4) runner emits the RunnerPlan to stdout with taskId, branchName, MINSKY_HOST_ROOT env, system-prompt overlay containing "Hypothesis self-grade" and the host's `pre_commit_command`; (5) ticket-key form (`AIFN-840` query) finds the task by title-substring match (case-insensitive); (6) idempotent re-run appends a second iteration record without crashing (both records have the right shape); (7) rule-#9 violation (task missing all 5 fields) exits 1 with `rule-9 violation`, all 5 missing field names listed, `iron` in the message; (8) not-bootstrapped run (no prior `minsky-bootstrap`) exits 1 with `not bootstrapped` + the bootstrap suggestion; (9) task-not-found exits 1 with `task "no-such-task" not found` + the available-IDs list. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (the test file is straight-line `describe` / `test` calls + small helpers `createFixtureHost`, `cleanupFixture`, `runBootstrap`, `runMinskyRun`, `runNode`). The runbook at `docs/cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-runbook.md` documents the operator-driven workflow for the live AIFN-840 fix (the v0 spawn path is operator-driven; v1 will auto-spawn). The runbook's Step 4 documents the manual iteration-record append for v0 — v1 will detect the merged PR via `gh pr view` and write `verdict: validated` or `regressed` automatically. End-to-end smoke 2026-05-04: `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/integration` exits 0 with `Tests 9 passed (9)`. The test file pattern is the inaugural example for the cross-repo-runner ecosystem; future host-specific integration tests (e.g., agentbrew, taskgrind, bosun) will mirror this shape — `<host-name>-shape.test.ts` under `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/integration/`.
