pure `detectFlakeRate(runs)` decision function over GitHub workflow runs identifies same-SHA failure→success pairs (Memon 2017 § 3, DETECTOR), surfaces a `FlakeReport` when per-job rate ≥ 10% AND ≥5 same-SHA pairs (DORA test-reliability mark, Forsgren _Accelerate_ 2018 Ch. 3); CLI shim ingests via `--workflows ci` (live `gh run list`, 30s timeout) or `--fixture <path>` (hermetic JSON); `--emit <dir>` writes a TASKS.md task-block draft + a workflow-patch instruction file per surfaced flake, idempotent re-emit, lighthouse-mobile downgrade #125 as canonical example; (vision.md § 11 — no flaky metric is a load-bearing claim; this lint is rule #11's mechanical ratchet, the difference between "we say flakes are forbidden" and "we mechanically catch them"); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; the lint surfaces the patch, never auto-mutates CI config); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; pure decision function over CI run history); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered as 4 sub-task experiments at `experiments/rule-11-flake-detection-{pure-fn,cli,patch-emit,ci-wire}-2026-05-04.yaml`; the parent epic was decomposed because operator discipline alone is insufficient — rule #11 needs a structural ratchet); TASKS.md `rule-11-flake-detection-lint` (parent tracker — the 4 sub-tasks `rule-11-flake-detection-pure-fn` / `cli` / `patch-emit` / `ci-wire` close to it); rows 9 + 10 (vision.md success-criteria — DORA test-reliability + token-budget honoring depend on a CI substrate that operators trust, which fails when flaky gates erode that trust).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Decomposed into 4 one-commit-sized sub-tasks (PRs #133/#134/#135/this PR).** PR #133 shipped the pure decision function (9 paired tests covering above-threshold / below / sample-size guard / empty / singleton / regression-not-flake / mixed-job / out-of-order / cross-workflow same-name). PR #134 shipped the CLI shim (`--fixture <path>` for hermetic CI; `--workflows <list>` for live `gh run list` with 30s timeout) + 7 more paired tests + 2 JSON fixtures under `test/fixtures/rule-11-flake-detection/`. PR #135 shipped `emitPatches` + `renderTaskBlock` + `renderWorkflowPatch` + `reportBasename` + `--emit <dir>` flag + 10 more paired tests (idempotency via `writeIfChanged` content compare; multi-report; CLI-emit clean+flaky paths). The parent task's pre-registered Pivot ("if workflow-patch generation proves brittle across YAML formatting variations, pivot to a human-readable instruction file") fired during sub-task 3 — the unified-diff form was rejected pre-implementation in favour of an instruction-file form, robust by construction; the lighthouse-mobile downgrade in #125 used this exact shape. This PR (sub-task 4) wires the `rule-11-flake-detection` CI job into `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: runs on `pull_request` only, `continue-on-error: true` so the detector itself stays informational (paradox-aware — a detector that fails the build on every detection becomes a flaky-feeling gate; per the parent task's Pivot, the lint is detection + scaffolding, not decision-making), shells out to `gh run list --workflows ci` via the runner's `GITHUB_TOKEN` (read access to the same repo's Actions, no extra secret), uploads `tmp/rule-11/*-task-block.md` + `*-workflow.patch` as workflow artefacts via `actions/upload-artifact@v4` with `if-no-files-found: ignore` so the green path stays quiet. `tmp/rule-11/.gitkeep` is tracked; the auto-emitted artefacts are gitignored so accidental local invocations don't pollute the repo. `docs/rule-11-flake-detection.md` documents the lighthouse-mobile downgrade shipped in #125 as the canonical "what success looks like" example so operators reading an auto-emitted patch know what shape merged work takes. End-to-end smoke against the local repo's `ci.yml` history: 26/26 vitest cases pass (9+7+10 from sub-tasks 1+2+3); typecheck + biome clean; `tasks-lint TASKS.md` clean. The full chain from "lint surfaces a flake" to "operator merges a fix" is now mechanical: detect → emit → review → merge. Rule #11 step 5 (auto-detect) is satisfied; the operator-discipline-only loop becomes a structural CI invariant. Tests cover all four failure-mode-table rows from TASKS.md `cross-repo-ci-action` plus the happy path: (1) `happy` → success; (2) `missing-self-grade` → failure with `pr-self-grade` row red; (3) `bad-anchor` (Medium URL) → failure with `anchor-primary-source` row red; (4) `experiment-unreadable` → experiment-rooted lints skip with reason; (5) `force-pushed` mid-run → neutral with "head SHA mismatch — re-emit dispatch". 15 vitest cases + 8 dispatch-emit cases all green; `node scripts/cross-repo-check-runner.mjs --self-test` exits 0 against the in-repo `gh` stub. The C3 fallback (host-side pre-push hook installed by `minsky bootstrap`) is already shipped, so operators are never blocked on C2 (~95 % coverage from C3 alone). The Pivot threshold (>10 % missing checks over 30 days once live, or sustained dispatch-delivery flakes → retire C2 to C3) is documented in the runbook and in the experiment's `pivot` field. the lint mechanises the "no scope without justification" constraint); Beyer, B., Jones, C., Petoff, J., Murphy, N. R. (eds.), _Site Reliability Engineering_, O'Reilly, 2016, Ch. 3 (error-budget discipline — known improvements before invented ones; the lint surfaces "invented without justification" rows mechanically); Beck, K., _Extreme Programming Explained_, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Ch. 17 (CI as the constraint enforcer); Munafò, M. R., Nosek, B. A., Bishop, D. V. M. et al., "A Manifesto for Reproducible Science", _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (pre-registration — the `experiments/<id>.yaml` gate operationalises rule-#9 pre-registration at PR-merge time); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (pure decision function at the centre, I/O at the edge); Meszaros, G., _xUnit Test Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 2007 (paired fixtures); rule-1 / rule-3 / rule-4 / rule-6 / pattern-index lints (the precedent shape this lint mirrors — same `--diff-base` / `--diff=<fixture>` / `RULE_*_PR_BODY_PATH` env-var contract); TASKS.md `scope-discipline-policy-lint` (the parent task this row closes) + `vision-plug-and-play-framing` (the dual-purpose framing PR that introduced rule #12 itself) + `host-transformation-checklist` (P0 sibling — the lint enforces the rule, the checklist documents the host-transform; both anchored on rule #12).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | **Pre-registered in `experiments/scope-discipline-policy-lint-2026-05-04.yaml`.** ≥6 paired vitest cases (currently 14) cover: justified-by-task (TASKS.md task block mentions path), justified-by-experiment (`experiments/*.yaml` body mentions path), unjustified (no resolution), human-approved via in-file comment, human-approved via PR-body comment, modifications + renames grandfathered, test/fixture files skipped, top-level workflow file justified, top-level script unjustified, paths outside eligible roots skipped (docs/, root \*.md, user-stories/), mixed batch (some justified, some not — only the unjustified are flagged), empty diff is a no-op, in-file opt-out wins over PR-body opt-out (more specific), bare path mention without an `**ID**:` block does NOT justify (the lint requires a real task block, not just prose). The CLI accepts `--diff=<file>` (a `<status>\t<path>`-formatted file) for hermetic fixture testing — `test/fixtures/rule-12/new-feature-without-experiment.diff` (negative — no justification → exit 1) and `test/fixtures/rule-12/new-feature-with-experiment.diff` (positive — this PR's own additions resolve via the pre-registered experiment + the `scope-discipline-policy-lint` TASKS.md task block → exit 0) are the two canonical fixtures. The CI job (`rule-12-scope-discipline`) runs on every PR + push, fetches `origin/main` (the diff base), pulls the PR body via `gh pr view`, and runs both the production lint (`node scripts/check-rule-12-scope-discipline.mjs`) and the unit tests (`pnpm exec vitest run scripts/check-rule-12-scope-discipline.test.mjs`); the job is wired into the `ci` aggregator's `needs:` list and into the gate-loop accepting `success` only. The Pivot path documented in the experiment's `pivot` field (status-R awareness for legitimate file moves; manifest approach via `package.json#minsky-public-surface` if substring-matching proves brittle) is the rule-#9 escape valve — false-positive trigger ≥2 / month sustained over a 4-week window. Rule #12 is now mechanical, not discipline-only. | 79  | `setup.sh --dogfood` (`setup-dogfood-flag` — closes the operator-onboarding gap surfaced by the 2026-05-04 user voice brief; consolidates `distribution/README.md`'s multi-line install snippet into a one-command invocation: detects OS via `uname -s`, renders `distribution/{systemd,launchd}/*` templates with `${MINSKY_HOME}` substitution via `envsubst`, drops them into the user-scope unit dir, idempotently loads/reloads the supervisor target, verifies `is-active`, prints operator's tail-logs / pause / status hints; idempotent re-run re-renders templates and re-loads — no-op on already-active supervisor)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Modeless one-step affordance (Cooper _About Face_ 2014 ch. on modal-vs-modeless interfaces — the operator's intent is unambiguous from the flag name; a one-command operator action mirrors OTP's `application:start/1` boot semantic per Armstrong 2007) + sidecar config consolidation (Hunt & Thomas, _The Pragmatic Programmer_, 1999, Tip 8 — "Make it easy to reuse"; the install snippet is documented prose that gets copied + edited; the flag is the reusable affordance) + reuse-existing-primitives (rule #1 — the unit-file templates + `lint-units.sh` smoke + the multi-line snippets all already exist; this row is the one-line orchestration)                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Cooper, A., _About Face: The Essentials of Interaction Design_, 4th ed., Wiley, 2014 (modal-vs-modeless — `--dogfood` is modeless: same affordance whether the supervisor is currently loaded or not; reload semantics handled internally); Armstrong, J., _Programming Erlang_, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (supervision tree — `enable --now` is OTP's start-the-supervisor verb; one-command operator action mirrors OTP's `application:start/1`); Hunt, A., Thomas, D., _The Pragmatic Programmer_, Addison-Wesley, 1999, Tip 8 ("Make it easy to reuse" — copy-paste-with-edits is not reuse; one-command flag is); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent: the unit-file templates + lint-units smoke + multi-line snippets already exist; the flag is one-line orchestration); rule #12 (vision.md § "Scope discipline" — Minsky-on-itself is the substrate the rule applies to; making the loop _easy to start_ is the rule's enabling step); user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.md (the umbrella user story for Minsky-on-itself; this row is the operator-side affordance the story's "Story" section assumes is in place); rows 4 (supervision tree) + 67 (tick-loop daemon v0) (the substrates this flag activates).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **Single bash block, OS-detected; no new code outside `setup.sh`.** The new `MODE="dogfood"` block in `setup.sh` (after the doctor block) detects OS via `uname -s`, renders the appropriate templates with `MINSKY_HOME=$ROOT envsubst`, drops them in `~/.config/systemd/user/` (Linux) or `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` (macOS), runs `launchctl bootout` then `bootstrap` (idempotent on macOS) or `systemctl --user enable --now minsky-supervisor.target` (Linux), confirms `is-active`, prints next-step hints (tail logs / pause / status / re-run). The `bootout`-then-`bootstrap` idiom is the macOS-side idempotency pattern (`bootstrap` errors if already loaded; `bootout` is a no-op when not loaded). `envsubst` is required (added to `--doctor`'s prereq list as a follow-up if it proves missing on enough hosts). The flag is invoked as `./setup.sh --dogfood` with no args; the multi-line snippet at `distribution/README.md` stays as the under-the-hood reference for operators who need to debug the install (custom unit dir, sandboxed shell). End-to-end smoke 2026-05-04: `bash -n setup.sh` syntax-clean; `./setup.sh --help` shows `--dogfood`; `MINSKY_HOME=$(pwd) envsubst '${MINSKY_HOME}' < distribution/launchd/com.minsky.tick-loop.plist` produces a plist with `<string>/Users/cbrwizard/apps/minsky</string>` for `WorkingDirectory`. Pre-registered in `experiments/setup-dogfood-flag-2026-05-04.yaml`. The Pivot path (per-OS install scripts at `distribution/{launchd,systemd}/install.sh` if the bash case-statement boundary proves brittle) is documented in the experiment's pivot field. | 70  | `novel/adapters/persona-spawner/` (`@minsky/persona-spawner` v0 — closes `persona-spawner-v0` (P0): `PersonaSpawner` interface (Adapter) in `src/index.ts` over `omc /team <persona>` invocations + `StubPersonaSpawner` test fake recording calls in-memory + `OmcPersonaSpawner` Strategy in `src/omc.ts` shelling out via injected `child_process.spawn` to `omc /team <persona>` against a per-task working directory, surfacing `<workingDir>/.omc/state/team/<teamName>/` for the `@minsky/omc-tasksmd-bridge` reader (PR #78) + pure `dispatchPersona(taskTags)` lookup in `src/dispatch.ts` mapping tasks.md task tags to OMC personas (`bug` → `engineer`, `feature` → `engineer`, `research` → `researcher`, `review` → `reviewer`, `refactor` → `engineer`, default → `engineer`); `selfTest()` follows the `green / yellow / red` lattice — `green` if `omc` is on PATH, `yellow` if not (Stub still works for tests; daemon falls back to dry-run path), `red` on a probe-time error; constructor-injected `spawnFn` + `hasBinaryOnPath` are the only I/O seams)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Adapter (Gamma 1994) over the OMC `/team` CLI dependency + Strategy (Gamma 1994) for swap-in-able persona-spawning transports — the v0 ships only `OmcPersonaSpawner`, but the seam keeps a future native CrewAI / Anthropic-Agent-Teams Strategy open without touching the consumer; test fake (Meszaros 2007) for the `StubPersonaSpawner`; role-based agent orchestration (Wooldridge 2009) for the dispatch table; let-it-crash boundary (Armstrong 2007) for the single `try { spawn(...) } catch { resolve(-1) }` at the supervisor seam in `runChild`; DI at the edge (Martin 2017) — `spawnFn` and `hasBinaryOnPath` are constructor-level, the only I/O surfaces                                                                                                                                                                                           | Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, _Design Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Adapter — interface over an external dependency; Strategy — `OmcPersonaSpawner` is one implementation, future Strategies plug in behind the same `PersonaSpawner` shape); Wooldridge, M., _An Introduction to MultiAgent Systems_, Wiley, 2009 (role-based agent orchestration — the dispatch table maps tasks.md task tags to OMC personas, closing the "society of specialists" promise from vision.md's north star — single Claude Code can't do this; OMC's `/team` mode is the existing tool, the adapter wraps it rather than reinventing a multi-persona runtime); Meszaros, G., _xUnit Test Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 2007 (test fake — `StubPersonaSpawner` records calls in-memory and returns a canned `{ exitCode: 0, durationMs: 100, omcStateDir: '/tmp/stub' }` so consumer tests assert call shape without forking a real subprocess); Armstrong, J., _Programming Erlang_, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (let-it-crash AT the right boundary — the single try/catch in `runChild` is the supervisor seam, returning `-1` rather than swallowing the error inside the function; a missed spawn must never crash the daemon — the daemon's tick cadence + supervisor restart is the liveness boundary); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (DI at the edge — `spawnFn` and `hasBinaryOnPath` are constructor-level, the only I/O surfaces; the rest of the class is pure given those seams); Hewitt, Bishop, Steiger, "A Universal Modular Actor Formalism for Artificial Intelligence", _IJCAI_ 1973 (each spawned persona is an actor; the dispatch table is the address resolver); Basiri et al., "Principles of Chaos Engineering", _IEEE Software_ 33 (3), 2016 (the README's failure-mode table — steady-state hypothesis "spawn() returns ok within one tick against a working OMC install", blast radius "a single spawn call", operator escape hatch "swap OmcPersonaSpawner for StubPersonaSpawner without touching consumers", 3 rows: omc-missing-from-PATH, OMC-session-fails-to-start, OMC-team-state-dir-not-produced); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — OMC is the existing tool; the adapter wraps it rather than reinventing a multi-persona runtime; opt-out comment in the README documents the no-existing-wrapper rationale); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; OMC's CLI is exactly that, behind `PersonaSpawner`); rule #4 (vision.md § 4 — every exported function carries `@otel adapters.persona-spawner.<verb>` JSDoc, with `@otel-exempt <reason>` opt-outs on helpers / test-fake methods); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; one `// rule-6: handled-locally — synchronous spawn-time error` opt-out at the spawn-time boundary, plus one at the probe boundary, plus one at the access-probe boundary); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — failure-mode discipline; the README's chaos table has 3 rows matching the brief's ≥3 requirement); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered Strategy-level pivot at `EXPERIMENT.yaml`: "if OMC's `/team` mode proves non-detachable, retire `OmcPersonaSpawner` only — the interface and consumers are unchanged"); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the chaos table is checked by `scripts/check-rule-7-chaos-coverage.mjs`, the pattern index by `scripts/check-pattern-index.mjs`, the @otel coverage by `scripts/check-rule-4-otel-coverage.mjs`, the rule-1 opt-out by `scripts/check-rule-1-novel-justification.mjs`); TASKS.md `persona-spawner-v0` (the parent task whose Acceptance this row closes) + `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` (the consumer that wires the spawner into the daemon's real-spawn path, now unblocked); row 62 (`@minsky/omc-tasksmd-bridge` — the reader that consumes the `omcStateDir` this Strategy produces); Munafò et al., _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the Strategy-level pivot was declared in `EXPERIMENT.yaml` _before_ code so a flaky OMC can't retroactively redefine "spawn API stable enough") | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No real subprocess in CI.** 24 paired tests across `src/{index,dispatch,omc}.test.ts` cover: Stub records 2 spawns in FIFO order with full opts, Stub starts empty, Stub `selfTest` is unconditionally green with `latencyMs: 0`, Stub returns canned default result on every call, Stub honours an overridden `cannedResult` from constructor, Stub `reset()` clears recorded calls; dispatch maps `bug` / `feature` → `engineer`, `research` → `researcher`, `review` → `reviewer`, falls back to `engineer` (default) for unknown tags, returns DEFAULT_PERSONA for empty tag list, walks tags left-to-right returning first match, accepts an injected dispatch table, PERSONA_DISPATCH_TABLE has ≥3 mappings; OmcPersonaSpawner invokes `omc /team <persona>` with cwd = workingDir + stdio = `['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']`, uses explicit teamName in `omcStateDir` when supplied, returns child's exit code on a clean close, computes `durationMs` from the injected clock, returns exitCode -1 when spawn throws synchronously (binary missing), returns exitCode -1 when child fires an `error` event (post-spawn ENOENT), `selfTest()` returns green when `omc` is on PATH, returns yellow when `omc` is missing from PATH, returns red when the PATH probe itself rejects. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8; the spawn path delegates to `runChild` + `runProbe` + `buildSelfTestResult` so each is ≤6). All exported functions/methods carry `@otel adapters.persona-spawner.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`adapters.persona-spawner.spawn`, `selfTest`, `dispatch-persona`, `run-child`, `run-probe`, `default-has-binary-on-path`); helper / type-guard / test-fake methods are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 with three `// rule-6: handled-locally — …` opt-outs at (a) the synchronous spawn-time boundary in `runChild`, (b) the PATH probe boundary in `runProbe`, (c) the per-PATH-entry `access` boundary in `accessSafe` — each documented with the chaos-table row it implements. The constructor-injected `spawnFn` is the only subprocess seam; tests pass a `vi.fn()` mock to assert argv shape without forking a real OMC binary. The `hasBinaryOnPath` seam is independent of `spawnFn` so `selfTest()` doesn't have to fork to check liveness — the lattice mapping (yellow on missing) intentionally distinguishes "OMC not installed" from "spawn itself broken" so the dashboard surfaces a soft-fail rather than a red on a fresh-install host, mirroring Avizienis 2004 status-lattice semantics in `@minsky/adapter-types` `aggregateStatus()`. v0 ships exactly one Strategy (`OmcPersonaSpawner`); future Strategies (native CrewAI, Anthropic Agent Teams) plug behind the same `PersonaSpawner` shape with no consumer-side change. The `omcStateDir` returned by `spawn()` is the path the `@minsky/omc-tasksmd-bridge` reader walks (`<workingDir>/.omc/state/team/<teamName>/`); when the child writes nothing (chaos row 3), the bridge returns `[]` — no spurious tasks land. Default `teamName = taskId` so concurrent spawns don't collide on the filesystem. | 67  | `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh` + `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs` + `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` + `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.test.ts` (`tick-loop-daemon-v0` — production tick-loop daemon orchestrator; v0 ships **dry-run only**: `runDaemon({ tickInterval, maxIterations, dryRun, mockClient, tasksMdReader, pausedSentinelReader, budgetGuard })` loops `pickTask → checkBudget → claim → spawnTickDryRun → emitOtelSpans` honouring `state/PAUSED` sentinel + `BudgetGuard.decide()` circuit-break; the bash bootstrap `exec`s `node bin/tick-loop.mjs --dry-run …` so the supervisor (systemd / launchd) sees the node PID directly; passing `dryRun: false` throws — real `child_process.spawn('claude', …)` is deferred to the follow-up `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` per the brief's pre-registered scope guard)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Periodic-task scheduling (Liu & Layland 1973 — `tickInterval` cadence + `maxIterations` cap is the periodic-task envelope) + MAPE-K monitor (Kephart & Chess 2003 — the daemon IS the running monitor that the MAPE pipeline assumes; full conformance after real-spawn lands) + let-it-crash supervision (Armstrong 2007 — the daemon never catches mid-iteration; supervisor `Restart=on-failure` is the respawn boundary) + Adapter (Gamma 1994 — `MockAnthropicClient` is the spawn-step seam, `BudgetGuardLike` is the budget-check seam, both injected)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Liu, C. L., Layland, J. W., "Scheduling Algorithms for Multiprogramming in a Hard Real-Time Environment", _JACM_ 20 (1), 1973 (the cadence model — periodic-task envelope with per-tick deadlines and budget-exhaustion halt); Kephart, J. O., Chess, D. M., "The Vision of Autonomic Computing", _IEEE Computer_ 36 (1), 2003 (MAPE-K — the autonomic manager assumes a _running_ monitor; this daemon IS that monitor); Armstrong, J., _Programming Erlang_, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (let-it-crash supervision — the daemon never catches mid-iteration, supervisor `Restart=on-failure` is the respawn boundary); Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, _Design Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Adapter — `MockAnthropicClient` + `BudgetGuardLike` are seams, the test fakes are stub implementations, the production wiring is a one-line swap); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (the bash bootstrap + `bin/tick-loop.mjs` are the I/O boundary; `runDaemon` is pure given injected I/O — `tasksMdReader`, `pausedSentinelReader`, `budgetGuard`, `mockClient`, `now`, `sleep`, `emit`); Beyer, B., Jones, C., Petoff, J., Murphy, N. R. (eds.), _Site Reliability Engineering_, O'Reilly, 2016, Ch. 3 (error-budget gating before each tick — the `BudgetGuardLike.decide()` step is the burn-rate check); Munafò et al., _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the dry-run-only scope was committed in the brief _before_ implementation, not post-hoc, so passing `dryRun: false` throws and the real-spawn path lands behind a follow-up PR); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; the spawn step + the budget step are both behind seams, and the test suite is stub-driven); rule #4 (vision.md § 4 — `@otel tick-loop.<verb>` JSDoc on `runDaemon`, `pickTask`, `claim`, `spawnTickDryRun`); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — exactly one `// rule-6: handled-locally — file-not-found is the documented graceful-exit path …` opt-out at the `readTasksMd` boundary); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — failure-mode discipline; the README's chaos table v0 row set adds PAUSED-sentinel + missing-TASKS.md + budget-guard-circuit-break); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered pivot at `EXPERIMENT.yaml`: "if dry-run mode reveals the orchestrator's data-flow assumptions are wrong (e.g., budget-guard's `decide()` shape doesn't compose with the loop's expected state), revisit before unlocking real spawn"); row 64 (`@minsky/tick-loop` v0 — the package this daemon extends); TASKS.md `tick-loop-daemon-v0` (the parent task whose Acceptance this row closes) + `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` (the documented follow-up that takes the dry-run guardrail off only after maciek-impl validates real-budget-state matches the loop)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | partial-real-spawn                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | **Dry-run only v0 (declared deviation, pre-registered).** The brief's primary path was a daemon that spawns a real `claude` (or `omc /team`) child process per iteration; v0 ships ONLY the dry-run path because real subprocess spawning has un-mitigated risk surface (env-passing, deadlocks on stdin/stdout, lease-then-spawn race) that's safer to land in a separate, narrower follow-up. Passing `dryRun: false` throws synchronously _before_ any I/O so the guardrail is mechanical, not policy. **13 paired tests** in `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.test.ts` cover (parent task brief): happy-path 4-iteration dry-run completes against a synthetic fixture (parent + child spans recorded — 4 `tick-loop.iteration` + 4 `tick-loop.tick`); PAUSED sentinel honoured within 1 iteration; missing TASKS.md graceful exit (ENOENT → `stoppedReason: 'missing-tasks-md'`); budget-guard circuit-break skips iteration with logged advisory; mock-anthropic 5xx → release-on-failure (status: `failed` with `5xx` in reason); `--max-iterations` cap respected; throws on `dryRun: false`; sleeps `tickInterval` between iterations (but not after the last); `pickTask` picks first unblocked unclaimed P0 task; skips claimed (`(@minsky-tick-loop)` marker); skips blocked (`**Blocked by**:` field); ignores P2 section; returns undefined when nothing pickable. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (`runDaemon` delegates to `runOneIteration` + `runClaimedIteration` so the loop body is dispatch-only, biggest function ≤10). All exported functions carry `@otel tick-loop.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`run-daemon`, `pick-task`, `claim`, `spawn-tick`); helpers / type guards / sleep are JSDoc-internal-only. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 with one `// rule-6: handled-locally — file-not-found is the documented graceful-exit path …` opt-out at the `readTasksMd` boundary (ENOENT → graceful exit; other errors propagate up to the supervisor — let-it-crash AT the right boundary). The CLI (`bin/tick-loop.mjs`) is the only I/O orchestrator; `runDaemon` itself is pure given the injected seams (`tasksMdReader`, `pausedSentinelReader`, `budgetGuard`, `mockClient`, `now`, `sleep`, `emit`). The bash bootstrap `exec`s into `node` so the supervisor (systemd `Restart=on-failure` per `minsky-tick-loop.service`; launchd `KeepAlive: SuccessfulExit=false` per `com.minsky.tick-loop.plist`) sees the node PID directly and a SIGTERM reaches it without a shell-wrapper detour. The `claim()` step is in-memory only in v0 (returns the lease shape; persistence to TASKS.md is the documented follow-up). v0 dispatches blocked-by ANY string match on `**Blocked by**:` — sub-string-aware "is _this_ blocker resolved?" semantics ship with the persistence follow-up because they need TASKS.md mutation, not just a read. The `BudgetGuardLike` structural type avoids importing `@minsky/budget-guard` itself (which transitively pulls `@minsky/token-monitor`); the production wiring will swap in the real `BudgetGuard.decide()` at `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn`-time. Conformance is `partial` (not `full`) for the same load-bearing reason as row 64's `tick-loop`: the daemon's MAPE-K monitor role is _partial_ until real-spawn lands; once it does, this row + row 64 + the follow-up row collapse into a single `full` MAPE-K-monitor row. **Sub-task 1/3 of `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` — `tick-loop-daemon-spawn-strategy`** introduces `novel/tick-loop/src/spawn-strategy.ts` (`SpawnStrategy` interface + `DryRunSpawnStrategy` synthetic + `ProcessSpawnStrategy` over `node:child_process.spawn` with last-4KB stdout/stderr tails + AbortSignal; never throws on non-zero exit — exitCode in result, let-it-crash AT the Strategy boundary per Armstrong 2007). The seam is reachable via `runDaemon`'s optional `spawnStrategy?` opt; production v0 stays defaulted to the legacy dry-run `tick(...)` path (no Strategy injected → `dryRun: false` still throws), so all 13 daemon tests pass UNCHANGED. **Sub-task 2/3 of `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` — `tick-loop-daemon-budget-guard-real`** wires the real `@minsky/budget-guard` `BudgetGuard` into the daemon via a thin facade `novel/tick-loop/src/budget-guard-facade.ts` (`fromRealBudgetGuard(guard)`, Adapter — Gamma 1994; the **executed Pivot** pre-registered in the task block, since the real `BudgetGuard`'s public surface is `tick(): Promise<BudgetDecision>` per the watchdog idiom — Beyer SRE 2016 Ch. 3 — rather than `decide()`). `bin/tick-loop.mjs` constructs the real `BudgetGuard` from a `MaciekTokenMonitor` (production — reads `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/<session>.jsonl`, the same data Maciek's `claude-monitor` reads) or from a `StubTokenMonitor` (when `--dry-run`, so the local smoke stays hermetic). The daemon never imports `@minsky/budget-guard` directly — rule #2's seam stays intact, the facade is the one-line bridge. After this sub-task the row's MAPE-K monitor role advances to **`partial+real-budget`** (the _Monitor_ phase now uses a real budget gate; the _Execute_ phase is still dry-run until sub-task 3 flips the spawn default). 14 paired tests in `daemon.test.ts` (one new — `real BudgetGuard wired via facade: circuit-break flips iteration to budget-paused` — drives the real `BudgetGuard.tick()` against a fixture `StubTokenMonitor` set past the 85 % circuit-break threshold and asserts the iteration flips to `budget-paused`, while a fresh window completes normally). **Sub-task 3/3 of `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` — `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn-flip` — SHIPPED**: production default flipped to `ProcessSpawnStrategy` (real `claude --print` headless subprocess per iteration — brief on stdin, response on stdout per `claude --help`'s documented non-interactive flag; the legacy `--resume` default — which opened the interactive session picker rather than reading the brief from stdin — was the bug fixed by `tick-loop-spawn-args-fresh-session`); dry-run is now opt-in via the `MINSKY_TICK_DRY_RUN=1` env-var control surface; the legacy `--dry-run` argv flag is retired from `bin/tick-loop.mjs` + `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh`. The supervisor unit files (`distribution/systemd/minsky-tick-loop.service`, `distribution/launchd/com.minsky.tick-loop.plist`) ship with `MINSKY_TICK_DRY_RUN=1` set during the safe rollout window — an operator drops that line / dict entry to flip to full real spawn (production default = unset). 16 paired daemon tests in `daemon.test.ts` plus a `tick-loop-spawn-args-fresh-session`-gated test in `spawn-strategy.test.ts` (`it.skipIf(!hasClaude)` on `which claude` — asserts the new `["--print"]` default produces a fresh non-interactive response, NOT a "Select a session to resume" picker UI; CI without `claude` skips cleanly, local with `claude` proves the real wiring). The pre-existing v0 production guardrail (`dryRun: false` without an injected Strategy throws) is preserved — the CLI always injects a Strategy, but a misconfigured caller of `runDaemon` directly still hits the throw. After this flip the row's MAPE-K monitor role advances from `partial+real-budget` to **`partial-real-spawn`** — the _Execute_ phase now runs against a real subprocess; full MAPE-K conformance lands when `user-story-001-integration-test-real` (now unblocked) drives the daemon end-to-end against a real `claude --print` on the self-hosted runner. Closes the parent `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn` epic. |

                                              | partial                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | **Pivot deviation (2026-05-03):** the brief's primary path was native `.shortcut.json` manifests Apple Shortcuts can import directly. Research (iOS-Shortcuts-Reference, sebj/iOS-Shortcuts-Reference) showed iOS 15+ uses **signed binary plist** for `.shortcut` files; hand-authoring or off-device generating signed binary plists has no documented public path. The brief's documented pivot path was taken: ship JSON config files + a step-by-step on-device build runbook (`distribution/shortcuts/README.md`). The autonomously-verifiable upstream half (URL / port / path consistency, JSON-schema fidelity, metric-id mapping to `SuccessMetric.id`, `/watch.json` returning the right JSON shape) is enforced by the smoke test (`test/shortcuts-json.test.mjs` — 16 assertions including per-file JSON parse + per-file schema check + URL invariant + watch-key drift + pause/resume symmetry) plus the server tests (`novel/dashboard-web/test/server.test.ts` — 5 new `/watch.json` tests on top of the 12 existing HTML tests). Total: 33 tests pass, 0 fail. The `/watch.json` route exposes **exactly four fields** (`tokens-remaining`, `last-task-status`, `constraint-of-the-week`, `paused`) — the 3-value glanceable cap (Card & Mackinlay 1999) plus the single boolean control flag for the pause/resume pair. Field naming maps 1:1 to keys via the typed constant `WATCH_METRIC_IDS` in `novel/dashboard-web/src/watch.ts` (rule #4 — every constant in source); the smoke test asserts each shortcut's `extract.metric_id` matches that constant (no drift between Shortcut and dashboard). Conformance is `partial` (not `full`) because the on-device step is **manual and operator-driven** — the v0 cannot mechanically verify that the operator actually built the Shortcut on iPhone (the verification half of `pnpm vitest run user-stories/005-three-numbers-watch.test.ts` is parent-scoped to user-story 005, not to this row). The pivot's next-tier rule-#9 pivot path (operator rebuild rate >1 / month sustained 90 days → escalate to `native-watchos-app`) is filed as the parent task's documented Pivot in TASKS.md. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (the smoke validator is split across 5 helper functions in `test/validate.mjs`, each ≤10). The pause/resume pair is symmetric: `pause` POSTs `{"paused": true}`, `resume` POSTs `{"paused": false}`; an assertion in the smoke test enforces the symmetry. **The pause/resume control endpoint (`POST /control`) shipped in PR #89** — `novel/dashboard-web/src/control.ts` accepts `{paused: bool}`, writes `${MINSKY_HOME}/.minsky/state/PAUSED` (or removes it), and is the sentinel the tick-loop daemon (row 67) reads each iteration. Both the data surface (`GET /watch.json`) and the control surface (`POST /control`) are now live; the on-device Shortcut build remains the only manual step. The Watch surface respects the 3-value cap from `ci-lint-watch-surface-cap` (P2) — `WATCH_METRIC_IDS` has exactly three keys, asserted by the smoke test. Default URL placeholder is `minsky.tail-scale.ts.net:8080`; the operator substitutes their actual Tailscale host per the runbook. M., "The Vision of Autonomic Computing", _IEEE Computer_ 36 (1), 2003 (MAPE-K loop — the orchestrator is the layer that turns a tick into operator-actionable artefacts: a draft branch + a constraints.md append + a research.md amendment proposal); Khattab, O., et al., "DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls into Self-Improving Pipelines", _ICLR_ 2024 (the rejected baseline whose "compile-time variant catalogue → A/B → winner picks" cycle survives in this orchestrator's flow, minus DSPy's runtime); Kohavi, R., Tang, D., Xu, Y., _Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments_, Cambridge UP, 2020, Ch. 3 (sustained-gain window — the rollout-draft only fires when the underlying `execute()` passes the 7 d sustained-gain guard); Ries, E., _The Lean Startup_, Crown Business, 2011 (oscillation guard — the orchestrator suppresses re-proposal of any variant that was rejected in the lookback window); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (the I/O boundary is the CLI, not `orchestrate(...)` itself); Beyer, B., Jones, C., Petoff, J., Murphy, N. R. (eds.), _Site Reliability Engineering_, O'Reilly, 2016, Ch. 17 (operator escape hatch — drafts never auto-merge); Munafò et al., _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the predicted gain is committed in the draft EXPERIMENT.yaml _before_ merge, so a future replay window can falsify or confirm it); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; the orchestrator never imports a vendor SDK, it takes an injected `PromptOptimizer` Strategy); rule #4 (vision.md § 4 — `@otel mape-k-loop.orchestrate` JSDoc on the exported function); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; the CLI's only `try/catch` is at the JSONL parse boundary, depth 1, with the documented graceful-degrade rationale); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — failure-mode discipline; missing experiment-store / missing constraints.md / corrupt JSONL all degrade gracefully without crashing); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered pivot at the parent task's `EXPERIMENT.yaml`: "if 90 d of orchestrator runs produce zero rollouts (gain threshold unreachable), drop sustained-gain window 7d → 3d OR widen the variant catalogue past v0's 3 mutations"); row 54 (`@minsky/mape-k-loop` core — the `tick(...)` cycle this orchestrator wraps); row 53 (`@minsky/prompt-optimizer` — the injected Strategy seam); TASKS.md `mape-k-prompt-rollout-orchestrator` (the parent task this row closes) + `experiment-tracker-knowledge-ingestion` (the consumer of the `--ingest-mode` flag, now unblocked)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No I/O in the orchestrator itself; all I/O at the CLI edge.** 6 paired tests in `novel/mape-k-loop/src/orchestrator.test.ts` cover: happy-path rollout (decision: rollout → branchSlug + EXPERIMENT.yaml carry the predicted gain at 4-decimal precision), oscillation guard suppresses repeat variant within window (decision: abstain, no rolloutDraft, reason mentions oscillation), sustained-gain failure abstains (in-window span < 7d → abstain, no rolloutDraft, reason mentions sustained-gain), no-op when no experiment verdicts available (no constraint → no rolloutDraft, knowledge.constraintsAppend includes 'no-op'), `--ingest-mode` skips Plan/Execute (rolloutDecision: null even with advisories present, but Knowledge phase still computes calibration MAE → researchAmendmentProposal fires), `--max-rollouts=0` suppresses draft on a passing rollout decision. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (`orchestrate(...)` delegates to `runTickFromArgs`, `maybeBuildDraft`, `renderExperimentYaml` — biggest function ≤8). All exported functions carry `@otel mape-k-loop.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`mape-k-loop.orchestrate`); helpers are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 with the JSONL-parse seam at the CLI as the single graceful-degrade boundary. The rollout-draft is **always a draft** — the CLI writes the EXPERIMENT.yaml to `tmp/proposed-rollouts/` and never opens a PR or pushes a branch (rule #7 — a load-bearing safety property; the operator review gate is the human-in-the-loop seam). The CLI's `--ingest-mode` is the consumer of `experiment-tracker-knowledge-ingestion`'s rule-#9 quarterly automation layer (vision.md § 9 — closes the loop from `validated-learnings.md` updates back into rollout decisions). Default `maxRollouts: 1` (one rollout per orchestrator invocation); the daemon's optional `mapeKOrchestratorIntervalTicks` integration (deferred to follow-up — `tick-loop-daemon-real-spawn`) will fire the orchestrator every Nth tick, with the cap matching operator review cadence. The injected `PromptOptimizer` is the seam — production wiring uses `AnthropicPromptOptimizer` (row 53), tests use `StubPromptOptimizer`. The orchestrator's calibration-drift threshold defaults to the `knowledge` phase's 0.5 (50 % MAE per Munafò et al. 2017); the `researchAmendmentProposal` only fires when MAE > threshold AND sample size > 0 (rule #7 — the "no signal yet" path is graceful). | 72  | `user-stories/006-runner-on-any-repo.md` (cross-repo-runner umbrella user story — pre-registers the rule-#9 contract for shipping minsky's full constitution against any host repo via a gitignored `.minsky/` sidecar at the host root and a `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT`-aware lint substrate; story ships before any of the 5 implementing PRs so the success/pivot thresholds cannot be retrofitted)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Sidecar configuration + parametric substrate root (Hewitt 1973 — the actor's "universe" is `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT`; the contract is `.minsky/vision.md` + `repo.yaml`) composed with check-run-as-asynchronous-boundary (Helland CIDR 2007 — `repository_dispatch` → minsky workflow → GitHub-API check-post; eventual-consistency window bounded by dispatch-delivery + workflow-run time) and supervised process spawn (Armstrong 2007 — the existing `ProcessSpawnStrategy` wrapped in `BudgetGuard`; making it parametric via `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` is the move that lets the same actor live in many systems); Helland, P., "Life beyond Distributed Transactions: an Apostate's Opinion", _CIDR_ 2007 (the substrate-locality boundary defines the consistency boundary; the minsky-side check-run is an asynchronous boundary with a bounded eventual-consistency window); Armstrong, J., _Programming Erlang_, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (supervisor wrapping the spawn — `BudgetGuard` is the SLA, let-it-crash on rule violations); O., Chess, D. M., "The Vision of Autonomic Computing", _IEEE Computer_ 36 (1), 2003 (cross-repo runs are heterogeneous experiments the MAPE-K loop ingests via the experiment-store; the runner is the _Plan + Execute_ phase of cross-repo work regardless of which repo the work happened in); Munafò, M. R., Nosek, B. A., Bishop, D. V. M., et al., "A manifesto for reproducible science", _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the umbrella experiment is committed before any of the 5 implementing PRs); Basiri, A., et al., "Principles of Chaos Engineering", _IEEE Software_ 33 (3), 2016 (the failure-mode table — 14 rows: not-bootstrapped / task-not-found / rule-#9 violation / budget-pause / hook-collision / spawn-without-self-grade / sandbox-leak / mid-spawn budget / dispatch-lost / broken-symlink / bootstrap-race / force-pushed / readonly-ignore-file / TASKS.md schema-drift); rule #1 (vision.md § 1 — don't reinvent — we wrap Claude Code's existing spawn, we do not replace it); rule #2 (vision.md § 2 — every dep behind interface; `MINSKY_HOST_ROOT` is the file-system-root interface; `BudgetGuard` and `ProcessSpawnStrategy` are reused unchanged); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; dry-run is the safe default, `--live` is the explicit opt-in; runner exits loudly on rule-#9 violations rather than papering over them); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — the failure-mode table has 14 rows, exceeding the brief's ≥3); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — this story IS the rule-#9 contract for the cross-repo-runner stack; the umbrella experiment block is the iron-rule pre-registration; per-step PRs each ship their own `experiments/<id>.yaml` at PR time once `experiments-directory-migration` lands); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic CI substrate is the minsky-side GitHub Action + the 12 host-aware lints, no LLM in the constitutional-gate chain); `docs/cross-repo-portability.md` (the operative classification this story acts on); `docs/cross-repo-portability/2026-05-04-classification-run.md` (the archived raw classification run preserving the rejected pivot path); TASKS.md `cross-repo-portability-doc` / `host-root-resolver-prep` / `minsky-sidecar-bootstrap` / `cross-repo-runner-v0` / `cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-integration-test` / `cross-repo-ci-action` (the 6-step implementing stack — step 1 shipped via PR #122)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | full (planned)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | **Pre-registered, no implementation yet.** Step 1 of 6 (`cross-repo-portability-doc`) shipped via PR #122; the substrate decision is documented; the runner is not yet built. Story status updates to `Implemented` only after step 5 (`cross-repo-runner-aifn-840-integration-test`) ships AND the metric (`cross_repo_runs_validated_pct ≥ 80%` over the trailing 30 days, after a 10-run warm-up) clears. Pre-registered umbrella pivot: if the metric stays below 50 % for two consecutive 30-day windows after the warm-up, the cross-repo architecture is wrong (not just under-implemented); pivot to thin-envoy mode (`BudgetGuard` + supervisor only, no constitutional gate) and retire the sidecar architecture as a load-bearing claim. Per-step pivots are smaller and surface earlier in each implementing PR's own `experiments/<id>.yaml` once the migration lands. The 14-row failure-mode table is exhaustive of the v0 acceptance — every row resolves either to a `loud-crash-supervisor-restart` (rule-#9 violations, sandbox leaks, missing bootstrap), a `circuit-break-and-notify` (budget-pause, dispatch-lost, scope-leak), or a `graceful-degrade` (hook-collision via chain-mechanism, schema-drift via tolerant parser, readonly-ignore-file via fallback to `.git/info/exclude`). Three of the rows (force-pushed, dispatch-lost, broken-symlink) document the asynchronous-boundary failure modes Helland's CIDR-2007 anchor predicts; chaos tests for them ship with the implementing PRs. The 12 host-aware lints (per `docs/cross-repo-portability.md`) plus rule-1's by-design exception are the deterministic substrate the constitutional-gate runs on; rule-6 is host-substrate-deferred to the host's ESLint. Filed before the implementing PRs precisely because rule-#9 is iron — pre-registration without execution is half a rule, but execution without pre-registration is post-hoc rationalisation. The umbrella experiment record will graduate to `experiments/006-runner-on-any-repo.yaml` once `experiments-directory-migration` (P0, ahead of the cross-repo-runner stack) ships the plural-experiments substrate. `selfTest()` follows the `green / yellow / red` lattice — `green` on a 2xx mock, `yellow` on rate-limit 429 (service is up but throttled), `red` on a network error; constructor-injected `fetch` (defaults to global `fetch`, no `node-fetch` dep — Node 18+) is the only I/O seam)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Adapter (Gamma 1994) over an external push-channel dependency + Strategy (Gamma 1994) for swap-in-able transports — the v0 ships only `NtfyNotifier`, but the seam keeps APNs / Pushover / webhook open without touching the consumer; test fake (Meszaros 2007) for the `StubNotifier`; let-it-crash boundary (Armstrong 2007) for the single `try { fetch } catch { return ok:false }` at the supervisor seam in `push()`; DI at the edge (Martin 2017) — the `fetchFn` opt is constructor-level, the only I/O surface                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Gamma, Helm, Johnson, Vlissides, _Design Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 1994 (Adapter — interface over an external dependency; the notifier is the operator-facing channel that turns a let-it-crash event into actionable feedback — story-001 acceptance #6 "a morning notification summarizes work done" is the load-bearing user-facing surface); Meszaros, G., _xUnit Test Patterns_, Addison-Wesley, 2007 (test fake — `StubNotifier` records calls in-memory and returns a fixed `{ ok: true }` so consumer tests assert request shape without a network); Armstrong, J., _Programming Erlang_, Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2007 (let-it-crash AT the right boundary — the single try/catch in `push()` is the supervisor seam, returning a failure shape rather than swallowing the error inside the function); Martin, R. C., _Clean Architecture_, Pearson, 2017 (DI at the edge — the `fetchFn` opt is constructor-level, the only I/O surface; the rest of the class is pure given that seam); ntfy publish documentation (<https://docs.ntfy.sh/publish/> — `Title`, `Priority` (1..5), `Tags`, `Authorization: Bearer` headers and the plain-text-body convention); Basiri et al., "Principles of Chaos Engineering", _IEEE Software_ 33 (3), 2016 (the README's failure-mode table — steady-state hypothesis "push() returns ok:true within 2s p95 against a reachable ntfy", blast radius "a single push call", operator escape hatch "swap NtfyNotifier for StubNotifier without touching consumers", 4 rows: ntfy.sh down, network partition, rate-limit 429, auth-token revoked); rule #6 (vision.md § 6 — let-it-crash; one `// rule-6: handled-locally — fetch rejection is the supervisor boundary` opt-out at the network seam); rule #7 (vision.md § 7 — failure-mode discipline; the README's chaos table has 4 rows, exceeding the brief's ≥3); rule #9 (vision.md § 9 — pre-registered Strategy-level pivot at `EXPERIMENT.yaml`: "if ntfy.sh proves unreliable — ≥1 outage / week sustained over 4 weeks — pivot to APNs or self-hosted ntfy on the user's Tailnet; Strategy-level only, interface and consumers unchanged"); rule #10 (vision.md § 10 — deterministic enforcement; the chaos table is checked by `scripts/check-rule-7-chaos-coverage.mjs`, the pattern index by `scripts/check-pattern-index.mjs`, the @otel coverage by `scripts/check-rule-4-otel-coverage.mjs`); TASKS.md `notifier-adapter-v0` (the parent task whose Acceptance this row closes); Munafò et al., _Nature Human Behaviour_ 1, 0021, 2017 (rule-#9 pre-registration — the Strategy-level pivot was declared in `EXPERIMENT.yaml` _before_ code so a flaky ntfy can't retroactively redefine "reliable enough")                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | full                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **No real network in CI.** 18 paired tests across `src/{index,ntfy}.test.ts` cover: Stub records 2 pushes in FIFO order, Stub starts empty, Stub `selfTest` is unconditionally green with `latencyMs: 0`, Stub `reset()` clears recorded calls, Stub `.calls` getter returns a stable readonly snapshot; Ntfy POSTs to `https://ntfy.sh/<topic>` by default with body + `Title` + `Priority` headers, Ntfy strips trailing slash on a self-hosted `serverBaseUrl`, Ntfy priority lattice maps `low`→2 / `normal`→3 (default) / `high`→5, Ntfy attaches `Authorization: Bearer <token>` when `authToken` is set, Ntfy omits Authorization when no token is supplied, Ntfy joins tags with commas into a single `Tags` header, Ntfy returns `{ ok: false, reason: 'http 429' }` on rate-limit, Ntfy returns `{ ok: false, reason: 'http 503' }` on 5xx without throwing, Ntfy returns `{ ok: false, reason: 'network: …' }` when `fetch` rejects, Ntfy `selfTest` returns `green` against a mock 200, Ntfy `selfTest` returns `red` on a network error, Ntfy `selfTest` returns `yellow` on a 429 (service-up-but-throttled lattice point), Ntfy `selfTest` sends a low-priority push with title `minsky.notifier.selfTest`. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8). All exported functions/methods carry `@otel adapters.notifier.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4 (`adapters.notifier.push`, `adapters.notifier.selfTest`); helper / type-guard / test-fake methods are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 with one `// rule-6: handled-locally — fetch rejection is the supervisor boundary; per rule #7 (chaos table rows ntfy.sh-down / network-partition / rate-limited), a missed push must never crash the daemon` opt-out at the network seam. The `fetchFn` opt is the only I/O surface; the rest is pure given that seam (Martin 2017). Caller is responsible for keychain lookup of `authToken` — same pattern as `MaciekTokenMonitor`'s `configDir` opt; the adapter never shells out to `security`. The `selfTest()` lattice mapping (yellow on 429) intentionally distinguishes "ntfy is broken" from "we're being too noisy" so the dashboard surfaces a soft-fail rather than a red on rate-limit, mirroring Avizienis 2004 status-lattice semantics in `@minsky/adapter-types` `aggregateStatus()`. v0 ships exactly one Strategy (`NtfyNotifier`); future Strategies (APNs via webhook, Pushover) plug behind the same `Notifier` shape with no consumer-side change.                                                                                                                                              | **In-process only.** No child processes; no real Anthropic SDK; no network. The 4-task synthetic fixture (`synthetic-tasks.md`) follows the canonical `**ID**: <kebab-id>` block-marker shape used in the real TASKS.md (and parsed by `scripts/check-rule-7-chaos-coverage.mjs`'s `parseTaskIds`). 11 paired tests in `novel/tick-loop/src/index.test.ts` cover: single tick succeeds (status: `completed`), 5xx maps to `failed`, span-per-tick (in-memory `SpanRecorder`), client-rejection mapped to `failed` without throwing (lease-expiry), malformed-output returns `success` status with garbage payload (downstream-MUST-validate), 4-task smoke runs within budget, span-per-tick across smoke run with one distinct task ID per span, budget-exhaustion halts the loop with `budgetExhausted: true` and partial results, `maxTicks` cap below `taskIds.length`, fixture parser extracts every `**ID**` marker, parser returns `[]` on malformed source. Cognitive complexity ≤10 enforced by biome (no function exceeds 8). All exported functions/methods carry `@otel tick-loop.<verb>` JSDoc per rule #4; helper / type-guard / test-fake / span-recorder methods are `@otel-exempt` with reason. Try/catch depth ≤1 per rule #6 with one `// rule-6: handled-locally — mock-client rejection is the supervisor boundary …` opt-out. Used by sub-task 3 (`first-integration-test-nightly-self-hosted`, dormant in TASKS.md) as the _unit_ the self-hosted runner runs at full 60-min cadence; this v0 ships the 10-min in-process smoke version. Default budget 600_000 ms (10 min), default `maxTicks` 4 (one per task type the parent's P2-task-throughput Acceptance exercises).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | The manifest is a `readonly CoverageEntry[]` of length 12 — one entry per row of `user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.md`'s "Failure modes & chaos verification" table. Status enum: `covered` (existing repo test — `file` + `testNamePattern` required), `self-hosted` (deferred to nightly self-hosted runner — `selfHostedTaskId` required, must resolve to a `**ID**: …` block in TASKS.md), `deferred` (cross-repo or otherwise out-of-scope — `deferredReason` required). Four assertions: (1) manifest length = 12 with rows 1..12 present; (2) every `covered` entry's `file` exists on disk and contains a test matching `testNamePattern` (case-insensitive grep); (3) `covered + self-hosted` ≥ 0.80 of total — the parent task's Pivot path counts both; (4) every `self-hosted` entry's `selfHostedTaskId` resolves to a `**ID**: <id>` line in TASKS.md (using the canonical block-marker shape so prose mentions don't false-match). Current breakdown: 4 covered (rows 1, 3, 4, 9 — `distribution/test-supervisor.sh`, `novel/handoff-spec/src/index.test.ts`, `novel/adapters/token-monitor/src/maciek.test.ts`), 7 self-hosted (rows 2, 5, 6, 7, 8, 11, 12 — OS-level fault injection: libfaketime, iptables, tc qdisc, dd, pmset), 1 deferred (row 10 — tasks-mcp lease semantics in a third-party repo). Coverage ratio = (4 + 7) / 12 = 0.917, comfortably above the brief's 0.80 threshold. The test runs in <1 s on any CI runner — it is an inventory test, not an OS-level chaos test. Sibling sub-tasks (`first-integration-test-mock-tick-loop`, `first-integration-test-nightly-self-hosted`) close the remaining decomposition: sub-task 2 builds the mock daemon for the 10-min smoke, sub-task 3 wires the nightly self-hosted-runner workflow that actually executes the 7 OS-level rows. The parent `first-integration-test` block in TASKS.md is now a tracker (P2, blocked-by all three sub-tasks).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
